tweets.text: The FBI’s Criminal Justice Information Services (CJIS) Division gathered for a memorial honoring fallen FBI agents and law enforcement officers at the annual Memorial Ceremony in Honor of Fallen West Virginia Law Enforcement Officers on Thursday, May 21. During the solemn ceremony, the #FBI and local law enforcement agencies paid tribute to all West Virginia law enforcement officers and FBI agents who have given their lives in the line of duty. The annual ceremony has been held on the FBI’s CJIS Division campus since 1995 and is a continuous reminder of the sacrifice made by those who gave their lives in service to protect our communities.

Every year, children are wrongfully removed from or retained outside the U.S. in violation of parental rights. International parental kidnappings (IPK) of U.S. children have been reported in countries all over the world.

Ahead of National Missing Children's Day on May 25, 2026, learn more about the FBI's roles in IPK cases in this Q&A with FBI Special Agent Ingrid Arbuthnot-Stohl of @FBISeattle —including how to report suspected IPK and how to keep your kids safe. 

🔗https://t.co/D9WRKBLkvW

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBISeattle: Armed Kent, Washington, drug trafficker sentenced to six years in prison for role in a family-led drug conspiracy

In March 2026, Treyvon Mitchell pleaded guilty to conspiracy to distribute controlled substances. During the investigation, Mitchell, 33, was identified as a co-conspirator in the Jackson Family drug distribution ring, and was a high-level member of the group which distributed more than 800,000 fentanyl pills throughout the United States, including in Arizona, Texas, Missouri, Montana, and Georgia. Over the course of the investigation, law enforcement seized more than 846,000 fentanyl pills, nearly 7 kilograms of fentanyl powder, 7 kilograms of cocaine and 29 firearms, as well as more than $116,000 in cash.

This case is being investigated by #FBI Seattle and its law enforcement partners, with support from @FBIPhoenix, @FBIAtlanta, @FBIKansasCity, @FBIDallas, @FBILasVegas, and @FBIBillings. Learn more: https://t.co/8vQygYZYdh

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBMilwaukee: Woman Wanted for Murder in Mexico Arrested for Illegal Reentry in Western Wisconsin

On April 2, 2026, the FBI National Threat Operations Center received a report that a woman living and working in Wisconsin was a suspect in a July 2025 homicide in Mexico. FBI personnel in Mexico coordinated with Mexican authorities to confirm that Alejandra Sanchez-Cuaquehua, 32, was a wanted fugitive, and that she had previously been removed from the United States and did not have permission to return. 

More information about the investigation by the #FBI and our partners: https://t.co/yKw5VHe633

tweets.text:
For 20 years, the FBI has welcomed children of fallen law enforcement officers to the FBI Academy during National Police Week—a tradition many students in the FBI National Academy now describe as one of the most meaningful parts of the program.

This year on May 16, 51 children arrived by police escort at Quantico as nearly 100 students from the FBI National Academy’s 298th session waited to greet them with cheers, games, carnival booths, snow cones, and service dogs.

Read the story at https://t.co/qu0JJVIWln

tweets.text:
#ICYMI from @FBISanFrancisco: Seven Chinese executives and four of the world’s largest shipping container manufacturing companies were indicted for conspiring to restrict the output of — and fix the prices of — nearly all of the world’s standard unrefrigerated shipping containers. 

The multi-year conspiracy roughly doubled the prices of standard shipping containers between 2019 and 2021, increasing the container manufacturers’ profits approximately one hundredfold during the COVID-19 pandemic and global supply chain crisis.

Read more: https://t.co/5RhaBi5E37

CASE UPDATE from @FBIAnchorage: A criminal complaint was unsealed in Alaska today charging a Canadian man with operating the KimWolf DDoS botnet. The U.S. complaint was unsealed following the defendant’s arrest in Canada by Canadian authorities. On April 10, 2026, U.S. authorities criminally charged Jacob Butler, aka “Dort,” 23, of Ottawa, with offenses related to the development & operation of the KimWolf botnet. KimWolf was a DDoS-for-hire service which infected over a million devices worldwide, including devices in Alaska. @USAO_AK @DoW_IG 

Press Release:🔗https://t.co/wdhOPogsfl

Learn about the FBI's role in combatting illegal DDoS attacks: 🔗https://t.co/Aa1PXL0gpB

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBIChicago: Chicago Federal Court Imposes Statutory Maximum Sentence of 15 Years in Prison for Defendant’s Illegal Possession of Loaded Handgun. 

A man has been sentenced to the statutory maximum of 15 years in federal prison for illegally possessing a loaded handgun. Dirk Hugo was arrested after he was found possessing a loaded handgun in his waistband and wearing a bulletproof vest.  Hugo had previously been convicted of multiple felony firearm offenses in state court and was legally prohibited from possessing a firearm.

Read more: https://t.co/XaPeo1STZo

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBILittleRock: Arkansas Middle School Teacher and Coach Sentenced to More Than 31 Years in Prison for Online Exploitation and Coercion of Minors

According to court records, Bradley Quillen, 31, a former Rogers Middle School teacher and coach, used the false online persona of a teenage boy to contact minor females via Snapchat and Instagram and coerce them to produce and send him sexually explicit images. The United States presented the Court with evidence that Quillen engaged in a scheme to target, groom, and sexually exploit hundreds of minor females in Northwest Arkansas schools and across the United States. 

Learn more about the investigation by the #FBI and our law enforcement partners: https://t.co/zAliDxOqKx

tweets.text:
Protecting the American people requires strong partnerships around the world.

FBI Co-Deputy Director Andrew Bailey visited Dakar, Senegal, to strengthen cooperation between U.S. and Senegalese law enforcement and security partners on shared priorities, including combating transnational organized crime, corruption, and other evolving threats.

During the visit, Co-Deputy Director Bailey met with senior government and law enforcement officials to reinforce partnerships that support intelligence sharing, strengthen investigations, and help protect both nations.

Strong international cooperation remains critical to the FBI’s mission to identify threats, disrupt criminal activity, and help keep the American people safe.

CASE UPDATE from @NewYorkFBI: Two Foreign Nationals Charged with Fraudulently Using the Trump Name to Scam Victims Across the United States

From at least in or about 2023 through in or about the present, Goran Spiridonov, Kristina Janeva, and others based abroad engaged in a widespread scheme to scam victims out of hundreds of thousands of dollars through the sale of so-called “Trump Bucks,” which they falsely claimed are valuable legal tender affiliated with President Donald J. Trump and the Trump Organization. In reality, these products are worthless and have no such affiliations. 

Spiridonov and Janeva are both citizens and residents of North Macedonia and remain at large. Read more about the #FBI investigation: https://t.co/bWMgye59ky

CASE UPDATE from @FBIChicago: Illinois Man Sentenced to 150 Years in Prison for Sexually Exploiting Children, Including Infants and Toddlers

For more than a decade, Matthew Kaufman enticed multiple children to engage in sexually explicit conduct and recorded it.  Kaufman coerced and enticed some of the children via the social media application Snapchat.  He stored hundreds of videos and images of the abuse on his electronic devices. Kaufman is a registered sex offender who was convicted in 2008 of a felony offense for possessing child pornography. The following year, he began the abuse that gave rise to this federal conviction and 150-year sentence.

Read more about the investigation led by the #FBI, with valuable assistance from our law enforcement partners: https://t.co/m38Q9hqbXw.

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBISeattle: Jamaican national sentenced to prison for lottery scam that devastated 73-year-old southwest Washington retiree

Roshard Andrew Carty first contacted the victim in 2020, claiming to be an employee of Publisher’s Clearinghouse and that the victim won $22 million and a car in the lottery, but that she needed to pay taxes and fees to access her winnings. Between 2020 and 2024, he convinced the victim to send more than $600,000 to various money couriers throughout the United States, who ultimately sent the money to Carty in Jamaica. Carty was indicted in November 2024. Following his August 2025 arrest in Jamaica, Carty consented to extradition to the United States. He pleaded guilty in February 2026.

Read more about the #FBI #fraud investigation here: https://t.co/ZhjxCaJC9G

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBIBoston: Former Director of Food Services for Plymouth Public Schools Pleads Guilty to Stealing Food and Equipment for Side Business

Patrick Van Cott admitted to a long-running fraud scheme that began in 2014 in which he stole food and commercial kitchen equipment to operate his private business, the "Snack Shack," on Sandy Neck Beach in Barnstable, MA. 

The National School Lunch Program (NSLP) is a federally assisted program administered on the federal level by the USDA that provides nutritionally balanced, low-cost or free lunches to public school children. In Massachusetts, meals for all students are free, and schools are reimbursed for meals through a combination of USDA funds and state funds.

Learn more about the investigation by the #FBI and our partners: https://t.co/fiig1zi619

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE FROM @FBIDetroit: Michigan Home Health Care Agency Owner Convicted of $1.6M Medicare Fraud Scheme and Kickback Conspiracy

According to evidence presented at trial, Ruby Scott, owner of a home health care agency, operated a multi-year scheme in which she bribed a hospital discharge nurse to unlawfully obtain Medicare patients’ confidential records and then used them to submit fraudulent home‑health claims. Scott caused approximately $1.6 million in losses to Medicare, which a witness testified drains the Medicare trust fund and could make it difficult for Medicare to pay on claims that are true and accurate.

The DOJ's National Fraud Enforcement Division is laser-focused on investigating and prosecuting those who commit fraud against the American people. Learn more about this investigation by the #FBI and our partners at https://t.co/t5o0TQl0sh.

He has not been seen or heard from since that date, and it is believed that foul play may be involved in his disappearance: https://www.fbi.gov/wanted/kidnap/isiah-jenifer

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBIMilwaukee: Eight Defendants Sentenced After Federal Investigation Seized More Than 175,000 Fentanyl Pills

The defendants operated a large-scale drug trafficking organization responsible for distributing hundreds of thousands of fentanyl pills throughout the Midwest. Several members referred to themselves as “GTR,” short for “Getting to Riches.” During the investigation, law enforcement seized more than 175,000 fentanyl pills and discovered that members of GTR spent thousands of dollars on luxury travel, expensive jewelry, luxury vehicles, designer clothing, and rap video productions. 

Learn more about this investigation by the #FBI and our law enforcement partners: https://t.co/6roXXE4LBc.

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBILouisville: Nigerian Man Sentenced for Conspiracy to Commit Money Laundering

Charles Nnamdi Emesim, 53, engaged in a decade-long scheme to receive and launder the proceeds of different internet- and telephone-enabled scams, including romance scams, government imposter scams, lottery scams, investment scams, inheritance scams, and medical expense scams. At least 23 individuals located around the United States were defrauded into sending a total of more than $700,000 to Emesim by mailing cash or cashier’s checks to his addresses, sending money transfers, or otherwise wiring funds into his bank accounts.

Learn more about the #FBI investigation: https://t.co/RbHwesiaSy

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBINorfolk: Chesapeake man sentenced to 17 years in prison after traveling to the Philippines to have sex with a minor

Through an online dating site, Dylan Joseph Knauer, 32, met a minor victim who resided in the Philippines. Knauer traveled there in April and May of 2025 and had sex with the victim, who was 14 years old. In October 2025, law enforcement learned that Knauer was scheduled to travel from Norfolk to Denver to Japan with a final destination of the Philippines. After boarding his flight from Denver to Japan, Knauer was removed from the aircraft before it departed, thanks to rapid coordination between #FBI Norfolk, @FBIDenver, and our law enforcement partners.

This case was brought as part of Project Safe Childhood, a nationwide initiative launched in May 2006 by the Department of Justice to combat the growing epidemic of child sexual exploitation and abuse. Learn more: https://t.co/qN8Zv0BCiw

CASE UPDATE from @NewYorkFBI: New York-Based Iranian National Pleads Guilty to Alien Smuggling and Receiving Child Sexual Abuse Material

Sharon Gohari, a naturalized U.S. citizen and who resided alternately in Iran and Nassau County, New York, solicited and received payments from Iranian nationals and others seeking to enter the U.S. unlawfully in exchange for arranging and facilitating their entry. During the investigation into Gohari’s illegal activities, law enforcement also discovered multiple videos on his phone depicting child sexual abuse material (CSAM).

Learn more about the investigation by the #FBI and our law enforcement partners: https://t.co/lbv7jO954J

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBISacramento: The founder of the Stockton-based "Punjabi Devils", a Hells Angels-affiliated gang, was sentenced to 64 months in federal prison for unlawfully dealing in firearms and possessing a machine gun. 

Jashanpreet Singh, 27, attempted to sell several weapons to an undercover officer, and a search of Singh’s residence resulted in the discovery of additional firearms. After failing to appear in state court, he booked a one-way flight to India. U.S. Customs and Border Protection notified the #FBI, and agents arrested him at SFO before he could board. 

This outcome is a testament to what's possible when federal, state, and local law enforcement work together.  Learn more: https://t.co/ssglJ4oZ9H

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBIWFO: California Man Sentenced to 78 Months in Prison for Role in $263 Million Cryptocurrency Scam

A multi-year federal investigation uncovered a social engineering enterprise that defrauded victims of more than $250 million in cryptocurrency. Members of the enterprise — based in California, Connecticut, New York, Florida, and abroad — played specialized roles that included database hacking, target identification, fraudulent phone calls, money laundering, and residential burglary. 

Marlon Ferro, 20, aka “GothFerrari,” broke into victims' homes to steal cryptocurrency hardware wallets. He also used fraudulent documents to open digital accounts that enabled the defendant and his co-conspirators to buy luxury goods, including an Hermès Birkin bag.

Read more about the investigation by the #FBI and our law enforcement partners, including @FBILosAngeles: https://t.co/czq5fDOyru

tweets.text:
🚨CASE UPDATE from @FBILosAngeles: Arcadia, California, Mayor Federally Charged with Acting as Illegal Agent of the People’s Republic of China

Eileen Wang has been charged in federal court with acting as an illegal agent of the People’s Republic of China (PRC). From late 2020 through 2022, Wang and Yaoning “Mike” Sun worked at the direction and control of PRC government officials. They coordinated with U.S.-based individuals to promote the PRC’s interests by, among other things, promoting pro-PRC propaganda in the United States through U.S. News Center, a website that purported to be a news source for the local Chinese American community, and they received and executed directives from PRC government officials to post pro-PRC content on the website.

Read more: https://t.co/J4teKIaKIK

CASE UPDATE from @FBIAlbany: Man Sentenced to 80 Years in Prison for Aggravated Sexual Abuse and Sexual Exploitation Involving Multiple Victims in Different States

Trevor Metterhauser, 41, of Pennsylvania, was sentenced to 80 years in federal prison for attempted aggravated sexual abuse, aggravated sexual abuse, sexual exploitation of children, and multiple counts of possession of child pornography. The aggravated sexual abuse and sexual exploitation charges involved two toddlers, one located in upstate New York and the other in Texas.

Learn more about the investigation, led #FBI Albany's Child Exploitation and Human Trafficking Task Force with assistance from @FBISanAntonio: https://t.co/3ZAEHaEyGo

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBISeattle: William Scott Morse is sentenced to 25 years in prison followed by a lifetime term of supervised release after pleading guilty to various sex trafficking offenses involving minors. 

In 2024, a minor victim posted a babysitting ad on Craigslist to which Morse responded asking if the victim would like to make quick money—but not for babysitting. Search warrants for Morse’s email accounts showed he was also conversing with a second minor, who had also posted a babysitting ad on Craigslist. 

Learn more about the investigation by the #FBI and our law enforcement partners: https://t.co/VwHuRq9ssX
tweets.text_resolved:

In 2024, a minor victim posted a babysitting ad on Craigslist to which Morse responded asking if the victim would like to make quick money—but not for babysitting. Learn more about the investigation by the #FBI and our law enforcement partners: https://www.justice.gov/usao-edwa/pr/defendant-sentenced-300-months-child-sex-trafficking

media.alt_text: News release | FBI Seattle | Defendant sentenced to 300 months for child sex trafficking | May 7, 2026

“Mr. Morse is a threat to the community who persistently targeted innocent and vulnerable teens looking for babysitting jobs online,” said W. Mike Herrington, Special Agent in Charge of the FBI Seattle field office. “He knew how young these victims were but continued to prey on them anyway. It is our hope that this long prison sentence will keep other young people safe from his crimes.”

tweets.text:
NEWS ALERT from @FBIBaltimore:  Foreign Operators and Technical Superintendent of M/V Dali Indicted for Roles in Key Bridge Crash

Special Agent in Charge Jimmy Paul joined partners Tuesday to announce that an #FBI investigation has resulted in charges against the foreign operators and technical superintendent of the Motor Vessel Dali, a 900-foot foreign flag container vessel that crashed into the Francis Scott Key Bridge on March 26, 2024. The Indian and Singapore corporations and a company official are charged with conspiring to defraud the United States and causing the death of six construction workers on the bridge. The indictment also alleges that the economic loss in this case is at least $5 billion.

Read more about the investigation by the #FBI and our partners: https://t.co/VqULjyaqoQ

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBISanDiego: Defendant Pleads Guilty in $48 Million Nationwide Book Publishing Scam Targeting More Than 800 Victims in the US

Michael Cris Traya Sordilla, a citizen of the Philippines, is the first of four defendants to admit his role in a scheme that targeted authors — most of them seniors — by promising to elevate their work to major publishing deals and Hollywood film adaptations, all in exchange for millions of dollars in fraudulent fees.

Learn more about the investigation by the #FBI and our law enforcement partners: https://t.co/VSsVGVMy12

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBIHonolulu: Mother and Daughter Sentenced to Federal Prison For Defrauding the CNMI Public School System, a Recipient of Federal Funds

SAIPAN, CNMI - Giselle Butalid, 34, worked for the CNMI PSS and used her position in procurement to deprive children of desperately needed resources while enriching herself and her mother, Clarissa Adlawan, 55. Investigators determined that Butalid forged documents to conceal “ghost” purchases and pocketed payments for materials that PSS never ordered. Upon receiving funds, the defendants frequently traveled to the Philippines, staying in luxury hotels, withdrawing large amounts of cash, and building a house.

Read more: https://t.co/uHrWgvJdOZ

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBIHonolulu: Ten Indicted in Drug Trafficking Conspiracies in Homeland Security Task Force Investigation

According to court filings, the drug trafficking organization sometimes referred to as H-Mob, had large quantities of methamphetamine shipped to Hawaii and used juveniles to store narcotics and firearms at the Puuwai Momi Housing complex. Investigators seized at least twenty pounds of methamphetamine and several firearms during the investigation. 

This prosecution is part of the Homeland Security Task Force (HSTF) initiative co-led by the FBI and HSI - a whole-of-government partnership dedicated to identifying and ending drug cartels, foreign gangs, and criminal organizations threatening public safety and national security.

Learn more: https://t.co/Z6p35vbTWK

CASE UPDATE from @FBISacramento: Former University Professor Convicted for Child Sexual Exploitation Offenses

Rodger Githens, a former university professor, initiated contact with a Grindr account controlled by an undercover agent, and discussed traveling to Fresno to meet the agent and the and the agent’s fictional 7-year-old niece. Law enforcement served a search warrant at Githens’s residence and seized multiple electronic devices with evidence that Githens’s was exchanging and commenting on videos of young children being sexually abused.

Learn more about the investigation by the @FBI and our law enforcement partners: https://t.co/xboSK78HSu

Read more about the #FBI investigation: https://t.co/kGfcQ3KQys

CASE UPDATE from @FBINewOrleans and @FBINorfolk: Virginia-Based Naval Serviceman Charged for Transporting Lafayette-Area Minor to Engage in Sexual Activity

The charges follow the successful recovery of the teen by the #FBI and the Lafayette Parish Sheriff's Office. The case arose from a parent reporting her 14-year-old daughter to have run away from home after the mother found her engaging in inappropriate communications with someone online claiming to be 18 years old. CASE UPDATE from @FBIMilwaukee: A multi-year drug investigation in Central Wisconsin resulted in 13 sentencings, following the sentencing of the final defendant last month in the Western District of Wisconsin.

According to court records, in February 2022 law enforcement officers with the Central Wisconsin Narcotics Task Force began investigating a methamphetamine and cocaine trafficking organization operating in Marathon County, Wisconsin. 

During the course of the investigation officers seized:
▪️12 + kilograms of cocaine
▪️285 grams of crack cocaine
▪️6 kilograms of methamphetamine
▪️1 kilogram of marijuana
▪️14 firearms
▪️$47,405 
 
Read more about the investigation by the #FBI and its law enforcement partners: https://t.co/NWBeuLRbMZ

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBISanAntonio and @FBIKansasCity: Texas Man Found Guilty in HSTF Case Involving Large-Scale Cartel Firearm Trafficking

Bobby Brandon Galvan aka “Puravidarecia,” 30, was affiliated with the La Nueva Familia Michoacana (LNFM) cartel and straw purchased numerous weapons, to include an AK-47 rifle that was recovered after it was used in a shootout between cartel members and Mexican law enforcement. On Feb. 20, 2025, LNFM was officially designated as a Foreign Terrorist Organization and a Specially Designated Global Terrorist entity by the U.S. Department of State. Galvan is one of 26 defendants indicted for charges ranging from firearms trafficking to illegal alien smuggling to money laundering.

Learn more about the investigation by the #FBI and our law enforcement partners: https://t.co/OBYU59DXsd

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBIChicago: Federal Investigation Into Violent Home Invasion Leads to Arrests of Seven Individuals

A federal grand jury in Chicago has indicted six men on robbery and kidnapping offenses for allegedly participating in a violent home invasion in Winnetka, Ill. in March.  A seventh defendant was arrested this week and federal charges are forthcoming. 

The charges allege that an individual in the residence was held captive for approximately an hour and that firearms were used to physically restrain them. The captors demanded access to a safe, computer, and online accounts holding cryptocurrency.

Read the indictment and learn more about the investigation by #FBI #Chicago and its law enforcement partners: https://t.co/4F0kOaV6LV

tweets.text: #OTD 90 years ago, former FBI Director J. Edgar Hoover personally arrested notorious gangster Alvin Karpis, aka "Public Enemy Number One." Karpis led a life of crime, but as the brains of the Barker/Karpis Gang, he remained elusive to law enforcement until April 1936, when word came that "Old Creepy" was in New Orleans. Hoover flew overnight to join the raid team, and they sketched out an arrest plan on a chalkboard. Once in custody, Hoover escorted Karpis to Minnesota to face kidnapping charges. Read more about the takedown: https://t.co/igOs24tB9k

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBICleveland: 25 Defendants Convicted in International $215M Scam That Targeted 1,000+ Victims

According to evidence presented in court, individuals, businesses, and other organizations in the U.S., were targeted and hacked by Nigerian-linked fraud organizations that defrauded more than 1,000 victims out of approximately $215 million. The scheme spanned 47 states and 19 countries. In total, 25 defendants have been convicted for their roles in this fraud and money laundering scheme, commonly referred to as a “business email compromise.”

Learn more about the investigation by the #FBI and our law enforcement partners: https://t.co/SgYsnNMjwQ

CASE UPDATE from @FBIHouston: Two Houston-area men get life for roles in murder-for-hire plot against wrong man

Michael Seery hired Ricardo Obando Jr. to kill a man he believed had an affair with Seery’s wife while Seery was in prison. Obando shot the victim several times in his neck, torso and hand, but the victim ultimately survived. In reality, Seery targeted the wrong person. The actual intended target had moved out of the residence where Seery believed he lived.

Read more about the investigation by the #FBI and its local law enforcement partners: https://t.co/7fQixbgApE

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBIMilwaukee: Former High School Dean of Students Sentenced to 18 Years for Producing Child Pornography

Law enforcement officers found sexually explicit videos of a minor female student on the phone of former high school Dean of Students Robert Gilkey-Meisegeier of Sun Prairie, Wisconsin. Upon further investigation, Gilkey-Meisegeier was found to have superimposed the faces of female students onto hundreds of pornographic images. Gilkey-Meisegeier's prison term will be followed by 25 years of supervision. 

This investigation was a part of Project Safe Childhood, a nationwide initiative to combat child sexual exploitation and abuse. Read more about the investigation by #FBI and its law enforcement partners: https://t.co/JVaXbXlqM3

tweets.text:
NEWS ALERT from @FBIHonolulu: Saipan Woman Sentenced to 71 Months in Federal Prison for Wire Fraud Scheme Targeting Multiple Victims

Sze Man Yu Inos, aka “Yuki”, defrauded multiple older women in Saipan and Guam, falsely claiming she came from a wealthy family in China, owned multiple businesses, and was successful investing in Bitcoin. Yuki treated the women to expensive meals and gifts and bragged to them about how much money she made investing in Bitcoin. After befriending the victims and gaining their confidence, Yuki requested money from these women and solicited investments in Bitcoin based on false pretenses. 

Read more: https://t.co/SoY0lrm79Y

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBISacramento: Man Sentenced for Narcotics Conspiracy in Prison Discovered Following the Death of USP Atwater Staff

According to court documents, Jermen Rudd III conspired with Jamar Jones, an inmate at USP Atwater, to introduce narcotics into the prison. Rudd mailed Jones a letter laced with narcotics, and when two correctional officers at USP Atwater opened the letter, they began to feel ill. After evaluation by medical staff, the Supervisory Correctional Systems Specialist was subsequently taken to the hospital where he passed away.

Read more about the investigation by the #FBI and its partners: https://t.co/OVsksNHFie

#ICYMI This week, @FBIAtlanta and its partners announced the results of Operation Sweet Silence - an extensive investigation into criminal street gangs in Columbus with ties to Mexican cartels.

CASE UPDATE from @FBILosAngeles: Canyon Country Man Pleads Guilty to Federal Charges, Including Facilitating Crime Tourism Group that Burgled Its Way Through U.S.

According to his plea agreement, Juan Carlos Thola-Duran directed associates, often members of crime tourism theft groups traveling from South America, to travel to various parts of the U.S. to commit thefts, including shoplifting goods from stores, burglarizing residences and commercial businesses, and stealing victims’ credit cards and debit cards. At least $23.9 million was lost as a result of the scheme.

Read more about the investigation: https://t.co/C8DfSRBPDq

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBIWFO: International Car Theft Ring Busted

A joint investigation has led to the indictment of six defendants who allegedly stole at least 20 cars and sold them to buyers in the U.S. and Ghana. The indictment follows a year-long investigation into an alleged D.C.-based auto theft ring that used electronic devices to reprogram cars to accept previously blank key fobs. Law enforcement is continuing to investigate the defendants in relation to the theft of more than 100 vehicles.

Officers also executed a search warrant this week at an automobile storage facility in Decatur, Georgia, which is suspected to be linked to the auto-theft ring.  

Learn more about the investigation by the #FBI and its law enforcement partners: https://t.co/hqMQItMavS

According to court documents, Jorge Pinon Armendariz, 47, directed a green laser at the Bernalillo County Sheriff’s Office Metro 1 helicopter while it was assisting ground operations. The laser struck the aircraft multiple times, including repeated hits to the pilot’s eyes.

Read more about the investigation by the #FBI and its law enforcement partners: https://t.co/02wgRWFwRB

tweets.text: Today, the FBI joins the nation in remembering the victims of the 1995 bombing of the Alfred P. Murrah Federal Building in Oklahoma City. The blast destroyed most of the nine-story building, injuring hundreds of people and taking the lives of 168, making it the deadliest act of homegrown terrorism in U.S. history. The FBI immediately responded to help, and the lessons learned shaped our approach to terrorism investigations in the years that followed. Learn more at https://t.co/yU7LM7on2b.

CASE UPDATE from @FBISaltLakeCity: Alien Drug Trafficker from Colombia Sentenced to Five Years’ Imprisonment

David Estiven Alvear Carcamo, 21, an illegal alien from Columbia was sentenced to 60 months’ imprisonment after he possessed and intended to distribute fentanyl in the District of Utah. This case is part of Operation Take Back America, a nationwide initiative to repel the invasion of illegal immigration, achieve the total elimination of cartels and transnational criminal organizations (TCOs), and protect our communities from the perpetrators of violent crime. 

Read more about the investigation by the #FBI and its law enforcement partners: https://t.co/O9iEOi4FvV

CASE UPDATE from @FBIChicago: Chicago Man Sentenced to 25 Years in Prison for Conspiring To Provide Material Support to Foreign Terrorist Organization

ASHRAF AL SAFOO was a leader of Khattab Media Foundation, a sophisticated online organization that swore allegiance to ISIS and created and disseminated threats and ISIS propaganda on social media and other online platforms. Many of Khattab’s postings included images of violence, celebrations of terrorist attacks and mass shootings in the United States, and encouragement for “lone wolf” attacks in western countries.

Read more about the #FBI investigation: https://t.co/qqvmDz11ZZ

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @NewYorkFBI: United Kingdom Citizen Sentenced to 10 Years in Prison for $97 Million Wine Fraud Scheme

James Wellesley, also known as “Andrew Fuller” and “Andrew Templar,” was sentenced to prison in connection with a scheme to defraud over 140 victims around the world of over $97 million. Wellesley posed as the Chief Financial Officer and Operations Manager of Bordeaux Cellars and solicited investors with promises of regular interest payments. However, the payments they received were sourced from new investment funds in a classic Ponzi-like scheme. Of the over $97 million solicited from victims, Bordeaux Cellars paid back only approximately $14 million before their Ponzi scheme unraveled.

Read more about the #FBI investigation: https://t.co/E1Ya8Su33e

CASE UPDATE from @FBISeattle, @FBIPhoenix, and @FBIHouston: First of two violent ATM bank robbers sentenced to ten years in prison

Seth Coles-Body, 24, of Houston pleaded guilty in February to four counts of bank robbery and two counts of attempted bank robbery for his role in a scheme to steal from banks by assaulting and threatening ATM technicians. Coles-Body and his co-defendant were identified as part of a robbery ring operating across the country including in Washington and Arizona.

Read more about the investigation by the #FBI and its law enforcement partners: https://t.co/XVJJMxx3e2

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBIDetroit: Man Who Declared “Death to You All” and Attempted to Commit Mass Murder Sentenced

Mack Davis, 23, was sentenced after pleading guilty to one count under the federal hate crime statute. According to court documents, Davis became infatuated with other mass murderers and planned to conduct his own mass murder. Among other things, Davis drafted a list of weapons and tactical gear for his plan. 

Read more about the investigation conducted by the #FBI and its law enforcement partners: https://t.co/V7GmFrailQ

We are grateful for the strong collaboration between our FBI Flint Resident Agency, the Owosso Police Department, the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives, and the Michigan State Police in disrupting this hate-inspired plot before anyone was harmed. Let this sentence serve as a notice that if you target others with hate or violence, you will face the full weight of justice under federal law.” - SAC Jennifer Runyan, FBI Detroit Field Office.

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBIIndianapolis: 13 Members of Armed Drug Trafficking and Dog Fighting Ring Sentenced

In 2023, investigators uncovered a large‑scale dog‑fighting and illegal gambling operation based in Indianapolis during the course of a broader drug trafficking investigation. The defendants played various roles within the dog‑fighting enterprise including 13 who maintained dogs for the purpose of fighting or served as handlers, responsible for provoking and motivating dogs during fights. Another 10 defendants remain to be sentenced in this case. 

To date, more than 100 dogs have been rescued from these criminals. Learn more about the investigation by the #FBI and its partners: https://t.co/xAzaB7R0mr

tweets.text: #OTD in 2013, two bombs detonated near the finish line of the Boston Marathon, killing three people and injuring over 500 more. The #FBI immediately responded, and in the days that followed, investigators processed more than 3,500 pieces of evidence and collected more than 33 terabytes of digital information, including photo and video tips from the public to support the investigation. At the same time, FBI victim specialists were deployed from around the country to support victims and their families. Today, a memorial stands at the site to honor the community's courage and the heroes who responded. Learn more about the investigation: https://t.co/RdwhYFcbEv

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBINewHaven: West Virginia Woman Sentenced to Prison for Lying to Obtain U.S. Citizenship After Committing Torture and War Crimes in Bosnia

Nada Radovan Tomanić, 53, served with the Zulfikar Special Unit of the Army of Bosnia and Herzegovina in the 1990s. When applying for U.S. naturalization in 2012, Tomanić lied about her service in a detention facility and her past criminal conduct — specifically, the crime of inflicting serious bodily harm under the Criminal Law of the Socialist Republic of Yugoslavia. Read more about the investigation by the FBI and our partners: https://t.co/rwcN1ZU9Fe

Members of the public who have information about human rights violators in the United States are urged to contact the #FBI at 1-800-CALL-FBI or https://t.co/tGqFRcJykB.

Rabih Hamdan, 41, admitted to creating and operating a scheme to submit false and fraudulent claims for prescription drugs that were medically unnecessary or not actually dispensed. In total, Hamdan and his coconspirators submitted false and fraudulent claims totaling at least $1.9 million over a five-year period.

The case was investigated by the #FBI and our partners at HHS-OIG. Read more: https://t.co/QFaPI6hErQ

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBICleveland: Former Ohio Senate Candidate Sentenced to Federal Prison for $4.5M Fraud Scheme

Jonathan Leissler has been sentenced to 4 years in prison after pleading guilty to three counts of Wire Fraud. Leissler embezzled millions from his employer and a local fraternal police organization to fund a lavish lifestyle and his election campaign. He was also ordered to serve three years of supervised release after imprisonment and pay $4,421,154.54 in restitution.

Read more about the investigation by #FBI Cleveland: https://t.co/RR2rcfxR29

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBIIndianapolis: Man Charged for Coercing Minor to Engage in Self-Harm and Possession of Images of Child Sexual Abuse

Billy Joe Holman, a/k/a “William Holman,” 26, met a minor female victim on a social media platform. He systematically targeted the victim—whom he knew to be 12 years old—by grooming, coercing, and otherwise compelling her to carry out his orders, including to harm herself. This case highlights the devastating reality of online exploitation. Know the signs. Talk to your kids. Report suspicious activity to https://t.co/tGqFRcJykB or 1-800-CALL-FBI. 

This case was brought as part of Project Safe Childhood, a nationwide initiative to combat the growing epidemic of child sexual exploitation and abuse. Read more about this investigation by #FBI and our partners: https://t.co/9U3CWqSdyk

CASE UPDATE from @FBIDallas and @FBINashville: Nine Men - Including Well Known Musical Artists - Federally Charged for Kidnapping at Music Studio in Dallas

Read more about the investigation: https://t.co/c9kvRXNMbj

tweets.text:
The FBI’s 2025 Internet Crime Report shows that Cyber-related crimes cost Americans nearly $21 billion in 2025. Last year, IC3 received more than 1 million complaints, with about 45% of those involving cyber-enabled fraud or scams, accounting for 85% of the reported losses.

The #FBI encourages you to #TakeaBeat and pause before responding to an unsolicited offer. If you suspect fraud, report it at https://t.co/hPS9NA6Fd2 or contact your local #FBI Office.

Read the full report: https://t.co/bl4qHXyU3Z

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBIStLouis: Man Sentenced to Prison for Sexual Assault on Grounds of Gateway Arch National Park

A federal judge sentenced Monte Derrell Boatman, 37, to 28 years in prison for sexually assaulting a woman in Gateway Arch National Park in 2023. Boatman grabbed the victim from behind, wrapped his arms around her and forced her into the bushes with a razor to her neck. He was arrested two days later by rangers with the National Park Service after a struggle that resulted in one ranger needing medical treatment.

Read more about the joint investigation: https://t.co/HNmSwyEnf5

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBIWFO: D.C. Man Sentenced to 205 Months for Role in 29 Armed Robberies and Attempted Robberies

Tavarus Thompson, 32, a previously convicted felon, was sentenced to prison this week for his role in a spree of dozens of armed robberies and attempted armed robberies of commercial establishments that included restaurants, convenience stores, and liquor stores in the D.C. metropolitan region during early 2022. Thompson acknowledged that he engaged in 29 of the robberies or attempted robberies. In all of them, at least one firearm was brandished including, many times, by Thompson himself. 

Read more about the investigation by the #FBI and our law enforcement partners: https://t.co/FW7snyj6d7

CASE UPDATE from @FBILosAngeles: Eight Arrested in Health Care Fraud Takedown, Including Owners of Hospices that Billed Taxpayers Millions of Dollars to Serve the ‘Dying’

This week, the #FBI and law enforcement partners in California arrested eight of 15 defendants charged in connection with nine separate health care fraud investigations focusing on hospice fraud and other schemes. Some of the defendants are health care professionals, including nurses, a psychologist, and a chiropractor. Losses to taxpayers exceed $50 million.

Read more about Operation Never Say Die at: https://t.co/mWOcmfUYfe

tweets.text: The #FBI and its partners spent nearly two decades hunting the Unabomber—an elusive killer who the world would later know as Theodore "Ted" Kaczynski. #OTD in 1996, the #FBI arrested the lone wolf bomber at a cabin in Montana. There, FBI agents sifted through a trove of evidence for nearly 24 hours before discovering a bomb under a bed. Today, 30 years later, a replica of the dwelling now stands at the FBI Experience in Washington, D.C. Watch video of its construction and learn more about the famous case at https://t.co/5Tzh6ayzQf

tweets.text:
#YourFBI is actively fighting transnational repression (TNR), a threat that can victimize members of foreign diasporas and their friends and loved ones, U.S. lawmakers who hostile nation-states want to control, or even members of the general public who these foreign governments consider to be dissenters. 

In response to this threat, @FBIPhiladelphia brought together 22 federal, state, local, and community partner agencies, creating the Bureau's first operational task force dedicated to recognizing, reacting to, and disrupting TNR threats.

Read more about this effort at https://t.co/xzZYZZTLwe

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @NewYorkFBI: Onetaste Founder Sentenced to Nine Years in Prison for Forced Labor Conspiracy

Nicole Daedone, the founder and former CEO of OneTaste, and Rachel Cherwitz, OneTaste's former Head of Sales, have been sentenced to nine years and 78 months in prison, respectively, for their roles in a forced labor conspiracy. From approximately 2006 through May 2018, Daedone and Cherwitz orchestrated a scheme to obtain the labor and services of multiple young women who had turned to OneTaste seeking healing and spiritual growth.  In reality, Daedone and Cherwitz used abusive and manipulative tactics designed to control OneTaste members.

Read more about the #FBI investigation: https://t.co/LYbTJtNtK4

tweets.text:
FRUAD ALERT from @FBIChicago: FBI Chicago is releasing photographs of two individuals suspected of impersonating federal law enforcement in order to defraud victims of money and personally identifiable information (PII). Recent reports detail that offenders have been known to create fraudulent scenarios, to include posing as bank employees who claim to be working alongside legitimate FBI agents, to gather personal and financial information on the victim. 

The #FBI urges the public to remain vigilant and exercise caution when communicating with anyone who claims to be federal law enforcement. 🚩Federal authorities will never ask for money or PII from potential victims or suspects when contacting them online or over the phone.

If you recognize these individuals or have been a victim, file a report at https://t.co/AMH9MOgvdJ or call 1-800-CALL-FBI. For more information, visit: https://t.co/QtJC3P7Pkh.

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBIChicago: Federal Law Enforcement Seizes Eleven Firearms, Ammunition, and Twenty Machine Gun Conversion Devices From Chicago Residence; Two Suspected Gang Members Arrested

During a search of a home occupied by Julian Torres, 19, and Brandon Osorio, 29, in the Chicago Lawn neighborhood on Chicago’s Southwest Side, agents discovered a short-barrel rifle and ten handguns, one of which was equipped with an extended magazine, the charge alleges.  Agents also discovered 20 machine gun conversion devices and assorted ammunition. The court was advised that Torres and Osorio are members of the 31st and Morgan Satan’s Disciples, and that the gang used the residence as a hub for its illegal activities.

Learn more about the investigation by the #FBI and our partners: https://t.co/mGHRw5YWiq

tweets.text:
The FBI, working closely with the Peruvian Minister of Culture and the Embassy of Peru, returned 48 cultural artifacts on March 17, followed by a repatriation ceremony on March 24 in Washington, D.C.

The artifacts—ranging from colonial-era paintings and textiles to ceramics and silver ornaments—span centuries of Peruvian history.

This effort is a testament to the coordination and collaboration from multiple international and domestic entities and reflects the strong and ongoing cooperation between Peru and the Unites States. @PeruInTheUSA

Read more about the investigation at https://t.co/PQYEuSwLRn.

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBISacramento: Paroled Murderer Pleads Guilty to Possessing Child Sexual Abuse Material and Being a Felon in Possession of Ammunition

According to court documents, in May 2025, law enforcement officers executed a search warrant at the residence of Marvin Mutch and seized various electronic devices containing tens of thousands of depictions of the sexual exploitation of children, some as young as 3 or 4 years old. Officers also seized a privately manufactured “ghost gun” containing eight rounds of ammunition. Mutch is prohibited from possessing ammunition because he is a felon who was convicted in 1975 of murdering a 13-year-old girl.

Read more about the investigation by the #FBI Violent Crime Task Force and partners: https://t.co/lsyeB3BCBS

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBIAtlanta: Sinaloa Cartel Member Sentenced for Distributing Methamphetamine

Jose Guadalupe Favela, a citizen of Mexico and legally in the US as a resident of Georgia, was sentenced to 188 months in prison followed by five years of supervised release. Favela, who is a member of the Sinaloa Cartel, was involved in a major methamphetamine and cocaine distribution organization out of Monroe, Georgia. During the investigation, Favela discussed his frustration with no longer receiving “busloads” of illegal drugs and said he was preparing for a visit by Ovidio Guzman Lopez, aka “El Raton,” a prominent member of the Sinaloa Cartel and son of Juaquin “El Chapo” Guzman, who was then a fugitive. 

Read more about the joint investigation by the #FBI and HSI: https://t.co/cbRJx4ZYEm

CASE UPDATE from @FBISacramento: A Fresno man will serve 14 months in federal prison and pay a $100,000 criminal fine for theft of government property.

According to court documents, Gurjeet Bath and other family members operated two trucking businesses. In 2020 and 2021, Bath applied for and received PPP loans totaling more than $1 million. To obtain the loans, Bath knowingly falsified records, and later used the funds to purchase two parcels of agricultural land in Fresno County.

Learn more about the investigation by the #FBI and our partners: https://t.co/1Xv5qtyiwo

Throughout its more than 100-year history, the #FBI has solicited help from the public to locate wanted criminals—especially those on the Ten Most Wanted Fugitives list.

Earlier this month, the FBI raised the minimum reward for each fugitive on the list from $250,000 to up to $1 million. All new and existing fugitives on the list are affected by this reward increase—and in some cases, the potential reward amount may be higher.

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @NewYorkFBI: Former Director of a Brooklyn Daycare Indicted for Stealing More Than $2.75 Million in Tuition Payments

Over the course of approximately four years, Murielle Misczak embezzled more than $2.75 million by directing parents to send tuition payments to unapproved accounts she controlled and transferring the funds to her personal bank accounts. Misczak spent over $600,000 in stolen funds on travel and entertainment, including over $350,000 on tickets to professional wrestling events, as well as hundreds of thousands of dollars on luxury goods and various personal expenses.

Read more about the #FBI investigation: https://t.co/y7FTRPlDAn

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBISanDiego: Eleven Defendants Charged in Takedown of Alleged Gang-Associated Drug Dealing

More than 200 federal, state, and local law enforcement officials executed search warrants this week in Escondido, National City, Ramona, and San Diego, California, as part of a coordinated takedown. Eleven defendants were arrested following a 16-month investigation by the #FBI San Diego Violent Crime Task Force – Gang Group and Homeland Security Investigations that targeted Asian Crips-affiliated drug dealers. The investigation included the use of court-authorized wiretaps, undercover agents, and confidential sources.

Learn more about the joint investigation and coordination between the FBI and our federal, state, and local law enforcement partners to protect communities from crime: https://t.co/p0lyQ1y6aa

CASE UPDATE from @FBISanAntonio: Armenian Man Extradited to U.S. Faces Charges for Role in Infostealing Malware Scheme

 An Armenian man made his initial appearance in an Austin federal court this week after being extradited to the U.S. on criminal charges related to his alleged role in an infostealer scheme. According to court documents, Hambardzum Minasyan allegedly conspired with others to enrich himself by developing and administering RedLine, one of the most prevalent infostealing malware variants in the world.

Read more about the investigation by the #FBI Austin Cyber Task Force and our partners: https://t.co/AHys0hjS39

tweets.text:
Alexander Gardine from the Turks and Caicos was sentenced to 240 months in prison followed by lifetime supervised release for the sextortion of a Missouri teen.

Gardiner pleaded guilty to one count of coercion and enticement of a minor in November. Gardiner met a 15-year-old Missouri boy via Snapchat. After multiple requests by Gardiner, the teen sent photos of his genitals to Gardiner, believing he would be left alone after doing so. Gardiner instead repeatedly harassed the minor via iMessage, Facebook, and WhatsApp, and threatened to send those images to the victim’s family and friends if the victim did not continue to produce child sexual abuse material for Gardiner. @FBIStLouis

Read More:  https://t.co/56gWC1Elr8

tweets.text:
The U.S. Justice Department participated in a court-authorized law enforcement operation to disrupt Command and Control (C2) infrastructure used by the Aisuru, KimWolf, JackSkid, and Mossad Internet of Things (IoT) botnets.

The operation was conducted simultaneously with law enforcement actions conducted in Canada and Germany, which targeted individuals who operated these botnets. The four botnets launched Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) attacks targeting victims around the world. Some of these attacks measured approximately 30 Terabits per second, which were record-breaking attacks. @FBIAnchorage

Read More:  https://t.co/1z6TmwHiPs

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBIHouston: Texas man sentenced for monopolizing international transit industry, fixing prices, and extorting competitors

Roberto Garcia Villarreal pleaded guilty to conspiracy to illegally fix prices and allocate the market for transmigrante forwarding agency services, conspiracy to monopolize the transmigrante market, and conspiracy to interfere with commerce by extortion. Transmigrantes arrange for and transport used vehicles and other goods from the United States through Mexico for resale in Central America. 

Read more: https://t.co/wfx7FJNigm

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE: Columbia Woman Who Coerced Minor Boy to Engage in Sex Sentenced to 20 Years in Federal Prison

According to court documents and statements made in court, Alyson Cranick, 44, first using text messaging and Snapchat, and then using the internet chat service Discord, coerced an 11-year-old boy to engage in sexually explicit conduct with her.  Several times during the summer and into the fall of 2022, Cranick induced the minor victim to leave his house after midnight to meet up with her.  Cranick sexually assaulted the minor victim during these meetings.

Read more: https://t.co/PwTGCJJXiJ

Trung Duc Lu has been added to the #FBI Ten Most Wanted Fugitives List. He is #wanted in connection with the torture, kidnapping, and murder of two Vietnamese brothers in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, in August 2014.  A third man was also tortured and kidnapped but managed to survive the attack. The FBI is offering a reward of up to $1,000,000 for information leading directly to his arrest.

Read more about this fugitive at https://t.co/lsueUuT8zw

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBIDenver: Carie Hallford of Colorado Springs was sentenced to 216 months in federal prison, three years of supervised release, and $1,070,413.74 in restitution after pleading guilty to conspiracy to commit wire fraud. 

Hallford, who owned and operated Return to Nature Funeral Home in Colorado Springs and Penrose with her then husband and codefendant, Jon Hallford, mishandled at least 190 bodies over four years and defrauded the Small Business Administration through fraudulent COVID-19 loan applications.

Read more: https://t.co/1KVeDeliRd

tweets.text: #OTD in 1990, two men dressed as police officers entered the Isabella Stewart Gardner Museum in Boston. After subduing the nighttime security guards, they seized 13 priceless works of art, making it the largest property crime in the world. The #FBI continues its search for the stolen artworks today, and you can help. Take a look at all 13 pieces, and if you have information on their whereabouts, contact the FBI at 1-800-CALL-FBI or submit tips to https://t.co/tGqFRcJykB.

tweets.text:
The Ten Most Wanted Fugitives list is a critical tool in the FBI’s efforts to bring dangerous fugitives to justice. It also underscores the power of public awareness in the pursuit of the nation’s most elusive and violent offenders. Since its inception, 540 fugitives have appeared on the list and 501 have been apprehended or located—many due to tips from citizens. To date, public tips have led to the arrest of 163 fugitives on the list. 

Hear more from FBI Assistant Director Heith Janke in yesterday's @ForeignPressCtr briefing on three new fugitives added to the list and how your help is critical to locating them and bringing them to justice. https://t.co/OuGfRNNg6C

See the full list at https://t.co/yZpW79oKgC and submit tips to https://t.co/tGqFRcJykB.

tweets.text:
Protecting Americans can begin before a crime even occurs. The FBI’s Behavioral Analysis Unit and the American Hospital Association have created a comprehensive resource to reduce violence and prevent threats from escalating into harmful action in health care settings. The guide outlines key principles of Behavioral Threat Assessment and Management to prevent and protect citizens and health care workers from becoming victims of targeted violence. 

Check out this guide and other resources at https://t.co/fnp0qj3uCE.

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBIHouston: Federal charges filed in fatal Houston Heights carjacking and shooting

Darius Dewayne Hall, 18, is in state custody and now also facing federal charges alleging he shot a victim on the evening of March 6, stole the vehicle she was driving, changed his clothes to avoid identification, and engaged in a vehicle and foot pursuit before his arrest. According to the allegations, the victim dropped off a friend after spending the day at MD Anderson Cancer Center, then parked the vehicle on the street and began walking back toward the residence. Shortly thereafter, witnesses heard a female scream, followed by a loud popping sound. The federal carjacking charge carries up to a life sentence or the possibility of death.

Read more: https://t.co/D0oVmkVAX0

tweets.text: The FBI’s Criminal Justice Information Services Division works with our partners to crush violent crime by improving the security and sharing of criminal justice information across the nation. This/last week, the CJIS Division brought together representatives from federal, state, local, tribal and territorial criminal justice agencies to discuss improvements to biometric identification, criminal history and crime reporting, and information security. Gathering input from our partners is just one way #YourFBI builds trust in our communities. Learn more about the CJIS Advisory Process and how these representatives make recommendations regarding CJIS systems and programs at https://t.co/p6H8fJ8snw.

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBIHonolulu: Honolulu Man Convicted of Child Exploitation Offenses, Including Production of Child Pornography and Sex Trafficking of Children

Darren Patrick Riley was convicted of all 12 counts related to his sexual exploitation of five minor boys on Oahu in 2019 and 2020. According to evidence presented at trial, Riley used the online app Grindr to meet the boys who ranged in age from 14 to 16 years old. Riley then engaged in a pattern of sexual abuse against them. This case was brought as part of Project Safe Childhood, a nationwide initiative to combat the growing epidemic of child sexual exploitation and abuse.

Read more: https://t.co/o7SGN1p6k9

"The defendant's heinous acts and repetitive exploitation of victims were put to an end with this verdict. This is the direct result of the relentless work of our agents and partners to protect the children in our communities. There is no corner of the internet or crevice on the globe where these predators can hide, as the FBI will use every resource at its disposal to bring them to justice." FBI Special Agent in Charge David Porter, FBI Honolulu

tweets.text:
An update on yesterday’s incident in New York near Gracie Mansion:

Two subjects were arrested on scene yesterday and remain in custody. 

FBI’s Joint Terrorism Task Force is investigating the matter with our partners at NYPD as well as the U.S. Attorney’s Office for the Southern District of New York. 

Preliminary analysis confirms one of the devices ignited and deployed by the subjects was an IED explosive. Tests remain ongoing for other devices.

We will continue to update the public as able. Thank you to the brave law enforcement officers who took quick action and responded to what could’ve been a deadly attack.

The public can report any additional information to the FBI by calling 1-800-CALL-FBI or submitting a tip online at https://t.co/tGqFRcJykB.

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBIChicago: Chinese Telecommunications Company Fined $50 Million for Conspiring To Steal Technology From Motorola Solutions

Beginning in 2006, China-based HYTERA COMMUNICATIONS CORP. LTD. recruited and hired Motorola employees and directed them to take proprietary and trade secret information from Motorola without authorization.  The stolen information was used to develop products for Hytera—at a fraction of the cost that it took Motorola to develop the exclusive technology—and competed with Motorola in the digital radio market through 2020.

Learn more about the #FBI investigation: https://t.co/XmckOdEAy3

On March 9, the U.S. designated Afghanistan as a State Sponsor of Wrongful Detention. The #FBI remains committed to working with our federal and international partners to return Americans who are being held captive home and bring their captors to justice. The Hostage Recovery Fusion Cell (HRFC) coordinates efforts dedicated to recovering Americans held hostage abroad and supporting the hostages and their families during and after release. We are steadfast in our determination to resolve these cases:

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBILosAngeles: Today, Acting Assistant Director in Charge Robert Molvar joined law enforcement partners to announce the arrests of 12 members and associates of Los Angeles's largest street gang. The 18th Street Gang allegedly controlled the city's McArthur Park as an open-air drug market, using tents to blend in with the homeless population and avoid detection by law enforcement. The #FBI and our partners seized approximately $80,000 in cash, 10 pounds of fentanyl, five pounds of methamphetamine, and six firearms today as part of the investigation.

Learn more: https://t.co/Q30f4Isy9n

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBIWFO: Alleged Sinaloa Cartel Boss Indicted on Drug, Firearm, and Terrorism Charges 

A federal grand jury in Chicago has indicted an alleged boss of the violent Sinaloa Cartel in Mexico on drug, firearm, and material support of terrorism charges. According to an indictment, Jesus Omar Ibarra Felix led an armed security group known as the Las Fuerzas Especiales de Chuta (FECH), which engaged in armed conflict on behalf of the Guzman faction of the Sinaloa Cartel, a U.S. designated foreign terrorist organization previously headed by Joaquin Guzman Loera, also known as “El Chapo.”

Learn more: https://t.co/AxoBKDkKNy

tweets.text:
Recently, @fbijacksonville joined federal partners in an operation at a major vehicle export hub along the St. Johns River where more than 500,000 vehicles moved through the port in 2025.

Criminal networks increasingly use exported vehicles as concealment methods for illicit goods. Instead of relying solely on traditional smuggling techniques, traffickers hide narcotics, currency, and weapons inside cars or containers declared as legitimate cargo. 

At a port that handles hundreds of thousands of vehicles each year, vigilance is constant.

Read more about the operation and the #FBI's work to combat transnational organized crime at https://t.co/jQlXITRYFu.

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBIPhoenix: Ex-Amazon Employee and Associates Sentenced in Multi-Million Dollar Fraud Scheme

According to court records, Abdullah Alwan was a former employee of Amazon, and used his knowledge of Amazon's systems to increase the standard rates for transportation deliveries by third-party carrier companies. Basheer Faisal and his brother Mughith Faisal operated a third-party carrier company and knowingly received the fraudulently increased transportation rates that Alwan input into Amazon’s system, working with him to defraud Amazon of approximately $4.5 million.

Learn more about the #FBI investigation: https://t.co/xc6vTnCWHp

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBIJacksonville: Massachusetts Man indicted and arrested for fraudulently using government seals, including the DHS, FEMA and FBI seals.

Mario Cesar Dos Santos, Jr. is the president of an organization called Chaplain Emergency Management Agency Inc. (CEMA), which he falsely claimed was a government agency endorsed by FEMA and associated with DHS and the #FBI. Dos Santos organized multiple CEMA chaplaincy training courses throughout the country, where he provided participants with ID cards, law enforcement badges, and course completion certificates containing the seals of various departments and agencies of the United States. 

Learn more about the joint investigation at https://t.co/t3hPG1mTGP.

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBICleveland: Two Men Indicted for Targeting Senior Citizens Across Four States in Money Laundering and Gold Bars Scheme

Federal agents discovered a scheme in which victims were falsely made to believe that they were communicating with their bank or federal agents from the #FBI, SSA, and FTC. The victims were manipulated through fear tactics and directed to withdraw funds from their savings and retirement accounts, convert it to cash and/or gold bars, and provide it to scheme participants posing as bank employees or federal agents. According to court records, Tejas Bhupendrabhai Patel, Navya Umeshkumar Bhatt and their co-conspirators physically traveled hundreds of miles to the victims’ locations to pick up the cash and the gold. 

Learn more about the investigation by the #FBI and our partners: https://t.co/U0dRHk6DHc

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBIIndianapolis: Indiana Man Who Targeted a Local Christian Church for Vandalism Sentenced to Federal Prison

Steven James Perkinson, 21, of Plainfield has been sentenced to 10 months in federal prison, followed by three years of supervised release. In 2024, Perkinson vandalized the sign of a Baptist Church by spray-painting a pitchfork and the numbers “666” over the display, and by painting over the sign’s cross symbol with an “X.” When questioned by FBI agents, Perkinson initially denied knowledge of involvement. Days later, after agents executed a search warrant on his cell phone, he admitted he targeted the sign because it belonged to a Christian church and stated he was motivated by his involvement in various Satanic groups.

Read more about the #FBI investigation: https://t.co/LZARzV2dq3

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBIHonolulu: Former Navy Petty Officer Sentenced to Over 8 Years in Federal Prison for Possession of Violent Child Pornography

Rumaldo Valdez, 22, was sentenced to prison this week after admitting to possessing child pornography on a US military base.  In 2024, #FBI and NCIS agents searched Valdez’s residence at the Naval Computer and Telecom Area Master Station Pacific in Wahiawa, Hawaii and found hard drives containing child pornography and child-abuse materials. 

The FBI’s search and arrest of Valdez stemmed from its investigation into “Greggy’s Cult” as part of DOJ's Operation Relentless Justice, a coordinated enforcement effort to identify, track, and arrest child sex predators, which has resulted in hundreds of child victims being located and the arrests of hundreds of child sexual abuse offenders.

Learn more about this case and the FBI's work to protect children: https://t.co/dmENLFH8xi

CASE UPDATE from @FBINewOrleans: Former Jail Nurse Guilty of Four Fraud Schemes and Possession with Intent to Distribute Oxycodone

Tonya Denise Brown, a licensed practical nurse, pleaded guilty to five felonies including four counts of Wire Fraud, with each count involving a different scheme. According to court records, in one scheme, Brown used a nursing job at the Tangipahoa Parish Jail to obtain money with false promises of assistance with state criminal prosecutions.

Read more about the investigation by the #FBI and our partners: https://t.co/39LUCrbs2r

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBILosAngeles: 24-year-Old Man Arrested on Federal Complaint Alleging He Groomed Girls into Producing Sexually Explicit Videos that Featured Self-Harm

According to court records, Bryant Najera Gonzalez used social media, direct messaging, and other means of communication to coerce, induce, and entice children to create and send him child sexual abuse material (CSAM). Gonzalez also discussed extorting his minor victims by, among other things, threatening to send sexually explicit images to the victims’ families. Based on Gonzalez’s online activities, law enforcement believes Gonzalez is associated with nihilistic violent extremist (NVE) ideology such as “764.”

Read more about the NVE threat and the investigation by the #FBI and our partners: https://t.co/Pzw6nWWQDt

CASE UPDATE from @FBILosAngeles: 24-year-Old Man Arrested on Federal Complaint Alleging He Groomed Girls into Producing Sexually Explicit Videos that Featured Self-Harm

According to court records, Bryant Najera Gonzalez used social media, direct messaging, and other means of communication to coerce, induce, and entice children to create and send him child sexual abuse material (CSAM). Gonzalez also discussed extorting his minor victims by, among other things, threatening to send sexually explicit images to the victims’ families. Based on Gonzalez’s online activities, law enforcement believes Gonzalez is associated with nihilistic violent extremist (NVE) ideology such as “764.”

Read more about the NVE threat and the investigation by the #FBI and our partners: https://www.justice.gov/usao-cdca/pr/downey-man-arrested-federal-complaint-alleging-he-groomed-girls-producing-sexually

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBIMinneapolis: Five Members and Associates of the Family Mob Street Gang Federally Charged

Three indictments and two criminal complaints were unsealed this week charging members and associates of the Family Mob street gang with drug trafficking charges. The Family Mob gang, which is based in south Minneapolis and originated in approximately the late 1990’s to early 2000’s, conspires to distribute fentanyl, crack cocaine, methamphetamine, and other controlled substances. In the past year, the area in the Family Mob’s territory has seen a large presence of open-air drug trafficking, weapons violations, shootings, and homicides.

Read more about the investigation by the #FBI and our partners: https://t.co/Dj6PRXlTrD

CASE UPDATE from @FBITampa: Eleven Gang Members and Associates Indicted on Racketeering, Attempted Murder, Murder Conspiracy and Drug Trafficking Charges

According to court documents, all 11 defendants are alleged members and associates of the Sex Money Murder-24K (SMM-24K) gang, which was active inside and outside of the prison system in the State of Florida and engaged in extreme violence to retaliate against fellow members for perceived violations of gang rules. 

Learn more about the investigation led by #FBI Tampa, and supported  by @FBIJacksonville, @FBIMiamiFL, and state and local law enforcement partners: https://t.co/iU7Yezqj8R

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBISacramento: A Bakersfield, CA man has been sentenced to five years in prison after pleading guilty to illegally possessing dynamite and manufacturing crystal methamphetamine.  

According to court documents, Matthew Henry Jacober was prohibited from possessing explosives following a 2021 felony conviction for making a destructive device without a permit. In July 2025, Jacober was found to be hiding 50 pounds of dynamite in a cave that was 10 to 15 feet from a travel trailer where he was residing, and where he was in the process of manufacturing crystal methamphetamine.

Read more about the investigation by the #FBI and our partners: https://t.co/lzDLaRT2Hi.

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBILosAngeles: Group Sentenced for Roles in Prolific Darknet Network that Sold and Distributed Narcotics Nationwide

The last of four defendants was sentenced this week for their roles in a conspiracy to operate accounts on several darknet marketplaces and sell methamphetamine, cocaine, MDMA, and ketamine to customers across the United States in exchange for cryptocurrency. One of the accounts, JoyInc, is believed to have been operating since at least 2018 and is one of the most prolific methamphetamine and cocaine distributors to ever operate on the darknet.

Read more about the investigation by the #FBI and our partners:  https://t.co/cFvSuwm2Jh

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBIHonolulu: Hawaii Man Charged with Threatening to Kill Federal Officer and His Family During Execution of Warrant for Drugs and Guns

According to information provided to the Court, Nehemiah Kealoha approached a Homeland Security Investigations (HSI) Task Force Officer who had assisted in executing a federal search warrant at the residence of an individual who had been placed under arrest for drug and firearms offenses.  As the HSI Task Force Officer was preparing to depart the residence and transport the arrested individual, Kealoha pointed at the officer and threatened to kill the officer and his family. If convicted, Kealoha faces up to ten years in prison and fine of up to $250,000, plus a term of supervised release.

Read more: https://t.co/kQ2Byr2J3d

CASE UPDATE from @FBIDallas: A citizen and national of El Salvador who was in the United States illegally, was sentenced to 45 years in federal prison for child exploitation.

Court records detail how Julio Chavez groomed a 12-year-old victim, ultimately convincing the child to send a sexually explicit video to Chavez.  Chavez also possessed hundreds of images and videos of child sexual abuse material on his cell phone.

Learn more about the investigation by the #FBI and our partners: https://t.co/CRHnv1RfTq

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBIHouston: Free Money Gang Members and Associates Arrested on Racketeering, Murder, Drug Trafficking and Firearms Charges

A joint investigation by #FBI Houston and law enforcement partners has resulted in federal RICO indictments against 20 alleged street gang members and associates. The charges are related to multiple offenses including a shooting at a shopping center in broad daylight. 

The case is part of the DOJ Criminal Division’s Violent Crime Initiative to prosecute violent crimes in Houston at the federal level. Read more about the charges and investigation at https://t.co/tPriwN4gf1.

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBIJacksonville: Former President and Vice President of Jacksonville Teachers Union Sentenced to Federal Prison for Embezzling Millions in Union Funds

From 2013 to 2022, Teresa Brady and Ruby George engaged in a conspiracy to steal more than $1.2 million apiece from Duval Teachers Union by selling back to DTU leave time that they had not accrued or earned, and by paying themselves unauthorized bonuses and fake reimbursements. They concealed their scheme by providing false information to DTU’s auditor and by signing each other’s checks. 

Read more about the investigation: https://t.co/Oj4Ce5rFBR

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @NewYorkFBI: Two Defendants Charged With Murdering Man at a Queens Intersection

Rafael Hernandez and Joibel Perez were arrested Tuesday morning for their alleged involvement in the murder of Akil Kornegay. As alleged in court filings, Hernandez and Perez operated a lucrative drug trafficking business, and a dispute over a drug debt arose between the defendants and Kornegay, who was their customer. In the early morning hours of February 26, 2021, the defendants pulled alongside Kornegay when he stopped at an intersection in Queens and fired multiple shots into the vehicle.

Read more about the investigation by the #FBI and our partners: https://t.co/b9oBBPmrcA

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBIDallas: A major fentanyl trafficker in Texas has been sentenced to life in federal prison for his role in controlling a drug trafficking organization that distributed drugs in Abilene and the surrounding counties.  

The sentencing of Kurtney Bernard Jones is the final phase of "Operation Top Shelf," a comprehensive narcotics investigation that involved seven local and federal law enforcement agencies and resulted in 31 traffickers sentenced to over 360 years in federal prison.

Read more about the joint investigation by the #FBI and our partners: https://t.co/IpVDzafeIf

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBIAlbuquerque: A New Mexico man is facing federal charges following an #FBI led drug trafficking investigation. 

The FBI Albuquerque SWAT team assisted in executing a warrant at the home of David De La Paz on February 5 and located him in his bedroom next to a loaded AR-15 style rifle on the bed. Agents recovered multiple loaded firearms, drug paraphernalia, and suspected fentanyl, methamphetamine, and 30 grams of cocaine. 

Read more: https://t.co/PJhjk1bdvy

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBIPhiladelphia: Department of Defense Employee Indicted for Moonlighting as a Money Mule and Laundering Millions of Dollars for Overseas Scammers

The indictment alleges that, from approximately July 2023 to December 2025, while employed as a Logistics Specialist with the Department of Defense, Samuel D. Marcus was in direct and regular contact with a group of Nigeria-based fraudsters, who engaged in a variety of wire fraud schemes that targeted victims based in the United States, including romance fraud, cyber fraud, tax fraud, financing fraud, and business email compromise schemes, to which victims lost millions of dollars.

Read more about the investigation by the #FBI and our partners: https://t.co/UDdKeitm8d

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBISeattle: A Washington man who posed as Snapchat tech support was sentenced to ten years in prison for a scheme to hack the accounts of multiple teens and access their private photos.

According to case files, Dylan Rex, 32, defrauded over 170 victims by triggering a password reset message and then asking the victims to provide the six-digit code to help them regain access to their accounts. Once the victim gave up the code, Rex locked them out of their account and stole the images he wanted. Many of the images and videos he stole he circulated on the internet, leading to harassment and extortion of the victims.

Read more about the investigation by #FBI Seattle, with support from @FBIBoston: https://t.co/ZCRzk15jBw

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBIAlbuquerque: Two men have been sentenced to prison for orchestrating a ransom scheme in which they held undocumented immigrants captive and extorted their families under threats of violence and cartel retaliation. 

The #FBI received a kidnapping complaint on March 1, 2025. Agents traced the ransom calls to a home in southwest Albuquerque where they discovered 11 undocumented immigrants from Guatemala, including one unaccompanied minor, as well as more than 20 cell phones and a ledger documenting smuggling activity. The victims reported that Isaias David Jose and Tomas Mateo Gaspar locked them in rooms, confiscated their shoes and phones, and threatened them with violence.

Read more about the investigation: https://t.co/6lSmmolR7H

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBIDallas: Two individuals have been sentenced to 70 and 80 years in prison for their roles in a conspiracy to sexually exploit a child. 

Using the title of Imam, Wisam Sharieff, 44, was an online instructor who specialized in Quran recitation and professed to have taught over 25,000 students worldwide. Blake Miller Barakat, 50, was one of Sharieff’s online students. According to court documents, Sharieff told Barakat that a person could become closer to Allah through achieving sexual pleasure. Both defendants coerced a child to engage in explicit conduct for the purpose of producing child pornography.

The case was brought as part of Project Safe Childhood, a nationwide initiative to combat the growing epidemic of child sexual exploitation and abuse. Read more: https://t.co/91wqD95ED8
