tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBITampa: Former NFL Player Convicted for $197M Medicare Fraud

Joel Rufus French, 47, owner of a marketing company and former NFL player, was found guilty in a conspiracy to bilk Medicare and the Department of Veterans Affairs out of nearly $200 million by selling patient information and sham doctors’ orders. French worked with overseas call centers that pressured elderly Americans, some of whom suffered from Alzheimer’s and dementia, to provide their personal and health insurance information and agree to accept medically unnecessary orthotic braces. In certain instances, the call centers altered call recordings to make it seem like Medicare patients agreed to the braces when they did not.

Read more about the investigation by the #FBI and our partners: https://t.co/I3OJVoUrVU

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBIJacksonville: Eleven Individuals Indicted in a Marriage Fraud Conspiracy

According to the indictment, the individuals recruited members of the US armed forces to enter into sham marriages to Chinese nationals for the purpose of evading immigration laws. The conspirators took photographs to create evidence that the couples were in loving, committed relationships. In reality, the conspirators had agreed that the US citizen spouse would receive a cash payment up front for marrying the Chinese national, a second payment when legal immigration status was obtained, and a final payment after the divorce.

Read more about the joint investigation with the #FBI and our partners: https://t.co/ySThfJmZtG

CASE UPDATE from @FBIAlbany: Massachusetts Man Charged for Assaulting Federal Officer with Dangerous Weapon

On January 29, 2026, members of the U.S. Marshals Service Fugitive Task Force went to a residence in Syracuse, New York, to execute an arrest warrant for Cedric E. Cundiff. Cundiff fled to a bedroom and when an officer attempted to open the door, Cundiff cut the investigator’s hand with a knife, causing two lacerations and a fracture. The charge filed against Cundiff carries a maximum sentence of 20 years in prison.

Read more about the investigation by the #FBI and our law enforcement partners: https://t.co/BgenmIfUNh

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBIMiamiFL: Florida Laboratory Owner Pleads Guilty to $52M Medicare Fraud Scheme Involving Genetic Tests

Sean Alterman, 38, owned and operated two laboratories that billed approximately $52 million to Medicare for false and fraudulent claims. Alterman made roughly $5.5 million from the scheme, much of which he received through shell companies. As part of his plea agreement, Alterman agreed to forfeit his Lake Worth, Florida estate and a 2022 Rolls Royce Ghost purchased with money traceable to the scheme. 

Read more about the investigation by the #FBI and our partners: https://t.co/VV4avEbu5J

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @NewYorkFBI: Four Members of Queens-Based Family Prostitution Business Sentenced after Being Convicted in Connection with a Sex Trafficking and Police Bribery Scheme

Blanca Hernandez Morales was sentenced to 35 years in prison Thursday for sex trafficking minors using force, fraud, and coercion. She was the final of four co-defendants to be sentenced. As proven at trial, since approximately 2002, the defendants, all of whom are related by blood or common-law marriage, participated in a sex-trafficking organization that caused young women and minor girls from Mexico to engage in prostitution in the US. The victims, including two minors, traveled to the US with false promises of employment and a better life.  When the young girls arrived in New York, they were forced by the defendants to work in prostitution.

Read more about the #FBI investigation: https://t.co/oQ1fOyqUdY

CASE UPDATE from @FBIStLouis: Illinois Church Official Arrested, Accused of Producing Child Sexual Abuse Material

This week, the #FBI arrested the president of the Central Illinois District of the Lutheran Church—Missouri Synod. A court-approved search of  Michael William Mohr’s home in Springfield found storage devices that contained videos of three juveniles in the bathroom. A search of a residence used by Mohr in Vandalia uncovered a hidden camera disguised as a wall clock and one disguised as a Bluetooth speaker. 

Learn more about the investigation: https://t.co/9VqwOmCWIf

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBIDetroit: Sitting Judge and Three Others Charged with Scheme to Steal Hundreds of Thousands of Dollars from Vulnerable and Incapacitated Wards

Four Detroit-area residents, including a sitting judge and a local attorney, were charged for their roles in a years-long scheme to embezzle money from incapacitated individuals. The indictment alleges that the defendants conspired to systematically embezzle funds, and to obtain and retain money for themselves that rightly belonged to the wards and the wards’ estates. 

Read more about the investigation by the #FBI and partners: https://t.co/FTLSznYp72

NEWS ALERT from @FBIChicago: A federal drug investigation led by the #FBI and Chicago Police Department has resulted in criminal charges against nine individuals for allegedly trafficking cocaine in the Chicago area.

A criminal complaint filed this week in federal court accuses Joel Andrade and Mora-Hernandez of conspiring to possess at least five kilograms of cocaine with the intent to distribute.  The pair also allegedly possessed two semiautomatic handguns in furtherance of their drug trafficking crimes.  The seven other defendants allegedly distributed cocaine last year in Chicago or the suburbs of Villa Park, Ill., Addison, Ill., and Rosemont, Ill.

Read additional details here: https://t.co/Ent4ndrYoW See less

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBIBaltimore: Jamar Geeter, 38, Jada Hasty, 29, and Khloe Gregory, 21, have been arrested by the #FBI and our partners and are charged with trafficking a minor across state lines for prostitution.

According to court documents, Geeter met the minor victim through social media.  When the victim said she had a difficult home life, Geeter promised to help her and convinced her to travel from NC to PA where she was picked up by Hasty and Gregory. The trio exerted control over the victim, including by destroying her personal phone and taking her social-security card and birth certificate.  Geeter also made threats of violence towards the victim and her family if the victim left or did not otherwise comply with their rules. Eventually, the victim was able to get away and ask a stranger to contact 911.

Read more about the investigation: https://t.co/5pBt2b3nBO

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBIJacksonville: On Monday, #FBI Jacksonville and law enforcement partners announced that Atharva Shailesh Sathawane, of India, was sentenced to 18 years in prison for his role in an international fraud scheme that targeted elderly victims and convinced them to liquidate their retirement accounts to obtain cash, gold, or both. 

Sathawane personally took, and then laundered, $6,615,484.66 in cash and gold from his victims, and then attempted to pick up an additional $1,363,395.98 in gold from two victims but was foiled by the intervention of a concerned friend of a victim in one instance, and by his arrest by law enforcement during the second attempt.

Learn more about the scheme and investigation here: https://t.co/pfpNyjm8Md

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBICincinnati: A doctor who tied up and repeatedly raped a woman in 1989 has been sentenced to 15 years in prison for making false statements to the FBI. 

The case remained unsolved for nearly 35 years, but the #FBI and our partners never stopped pursuing justice. In September 2024, it was confirmed that DNA taken from the coffee cup of Frederick Louis Tanzer, now 67, matched the semen left by the attacker. When approached, Tanzer made several false statements. 

Read more about the investigation: https://t.co/22iId7ejWc

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBIChicago: Man Sentenced to 17 Years in Prison for Two Carjackings

In 2022, Edmund Singleton and two co-defendants drove a stolen car to a gas station where they violently took a sedan from a driver who was pumping gas. A few hours later, they attempted to take a BMW from a driver who was also pumping gas and walked with crutches.  The victim fell to the ground as the trio tried to drive away, but they gave up when they couldn’t operate the specially designed car. A chase involving police helicopters ensued.

Read more about the arrests and the joint investigation by the #FBI and our law enforcement partners: https://t.co/95eQLDXefJ

CASE UPDATE from @FBIBoston: Two New York Men Plead Guilty to Mortgage Fraud Conspiracy To Buy Illegal Marijuana Grow Houses

Tony Liang of Brooklyn and Yongliang Deng of Queens fraudulently obtained $542,200 in residential mortgage loans to buy multiple houses in Maine. tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE: #FBI Ten Most Wanted Fugitive Ryan Wedding has been arrested in Mexico and brought back to the United States. 

Wedding was wanted for allegedly running and participating in a transnational drug trafficking operation that routinely shipped hundreds of kilograms of cocaine from Colombia, through Mexico and Southern California, to Canada, and other locations in the United States. Additionally, it is alleged that Wedding was involved in orchestrating multiple murders in furtherance of these drug crimes.

Follow @FBILosAngeles for additional information and case updates.

tweets.text:
The FBI is offering a reward of up to $100,000 for information leading to the recovery of stolen government property and/or the arrest of individuals responsible for the destruction and theft of government property in Minneapolis, Minnesota.

On January 14, 2026, at approximately 7:00 p.m., FBI personnel responded to an assault on a federal officer investigation in North Minneapolis. Several government vehicles were subsequently vandalized and broken into, and government property was stolen from inside the vehicles.

The FBI is asking for any photos, video, or other digital footage from the scene - these can be submitted at https://t.co/xeHbsvlac7.

If you have any information on this case, please contact the FBI at 1-800-CALL-FBI or submit a tip online at https://t.co/tGqFRcJykB.

tweets.text:
Operational technology (OT) systems that were once isolated are now more interconnected than ever, delivering efficiency and real-time visibility but also increasing #cyber risks.

Exposed and insecure OT systems—often connected to enterprise systems, third-party vendors, and the internet—are known to be targeted by both opportunistic and highly capable actors, who can gain access to sensitive OT devices and networks.

This guidance document, which provides secure connectivity principles for OT, was led by @NCSC and co-authored by #FBI, @CISAgov, and other partners. Learn how your organization should design, secure, and manage connectivity in OT. https://t.co/Qn5fVhn3T8
tweets.text_resolved:

tweets.text:
Every day, law enforcement and citizens turn to the FBI’s Criminal Justice Information Services Division for our services. In 2025, the National Threat Operations Center received more than 1 million calls about threats or other potential crimes. 

Law enforcement partners turned to the National Crime Information Center more than 10.7 million times a day. The division is transforming the way it works, to serve you in a quicker manner. 

Learn more about the division in action at https://t.co/wKKxZ8Fgyz.

tweets.text:
In December 2025, FBI Victim Services celebrated the retirement of Gio, one of the #FBI's first Crisis Response Canines. 

Gio was the first federal facility dog to support a victim during testimony in a federal courtroom. Since joining the Bureau in 2015, Gio and his paw-tner Wally have assisted over 4,100 victims and families and deployed to 12 mass violence incidents. 

The Crisis Response Canines program continues to be active with the FBI's newest recruits: Taz and Peg. Learn more about our CRCs and how they support the FBI's mission at https://t.co/Hf19UOcBBF

tweets.text:
The #FBI is warning think tanks, academia, and foreign policy organizations that North Korean cyber actors are using malicious QR codes in spearphishing campaigns.

These actors spoof trusted figures in the foreign policy space and embed malicious URLs inside QR codes, forcing victims to pivot from workstations to mobile browsers, bypassing email security controls.

We urge potentially targeted organizations to protect themselves by reviewing this new report: https://t.co/swY6L61tYW

tweets.text:
We are excited to announce the launch of the FBI’s new technology strategy. This strategy is guided by three fundamental goals: 

1. Built for the mission—we are collaborating on strategic investments to improve our systems and tools while adopting IT innovations that address the FBI’s unique mission needs.
  
2. Easy to find and access—we are committed to delivering readily accessible and highly effective technology and data that enables the FBI to rapidly adapt to emerging threats.
  
3. Fast and reliable—we are dedicating our modernization efforts to develop agile enterprise solutions while securing our infrastructure and data.

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBINewHaven: Ecuadorian National Charged with Child Exploitation Offenses

Jose Israel Tenesaca Mejia, 21, a citizen of Ecuador residing in Connecticut, has been charged with federal child exploitation offenses. As alleged, the Stamford Police Department secured an arrest warrant for Mejia, who subsequently fled Connecticut with a 14-year-old girl. The FBI in New Haven contacted the FBI in Minneapolis and relayed information about Mejia’s active arrest warrant, and that his iPhone had been tracked to a specific location in Minneapolis. Investigators surveilled the vicinity of the location, identified both Mejia and the minor victim, and arrested Mejia.

Read more about the investigation by @FBI New Haven, @FBIMinneapolis and partners: https://t.co/KJ6IGRWsTr.

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBIPhoenix: Arizona Man Sentenced to 23 Years in Prison for Second Degree Murder

In January, Brandon Hyden, 40, murdered an unarmed victim by shooting him six times in a parking lot at a business in Tuba City, Arizona. Approximately thirty minutes later, Hyden shot another unarmed victim four times in a residential area. Hyden is an enrolled member of the Navajo Nation.

Read more about the investigation by the #FBI and our partners: https://t.co/JyZuSGEKAD

tweets.text:
As we welcome 2026, look back at how @FBIPhoenix helped protect its communities and keep Americans safe in 2025. 

"As 2025 comes to a close, FBI Phoenix is looking back on a few successful operations and cases. In coordination with our federal, state, tribal and local partners FBI Phoenix arrested violent criminals, rescued minor victims, protected against acts of terrorism, disrupted cyber-attacks, and combatted complex financial fraud. But we couldn’t do this without you - partnerships with not only law enforcement, but the private and public sectors continue to play a key role in our success. Heading into the new year, know that we are here and ready to serve and protect all citizens of Arizona."

tweets.text_resolved:
As we welcome 2026, look back at how @FBIAnchorage helped protect its communities and keep Americans safe in 2025. 

"As the year comes to a close, we thank our Alaska community for your trust and support. Here is how FBI Anchorage, together with our partners, helped protect communities throughout Alaska in 2025."

media.alt_text: FBI Anchorage in 2025 achieved approximately 105 arrests, 52 convictions, and 59 sentencings with major seizures of firearms and drugs. FBI Anchorage also seized an entire hotel, ending alleged drug operations at the business. // FBI SWAT personnel are in the background. // A white FBI logo is in the lower right corner.

FBI Anchorage in 2025 conducted SABT deployments to mitigate hazardous and explosive threats in Alaska, worked behind-the-scenes in a 24/7 command post to support the security of a historic presidential summit, seized over $5.6 million in cryptocurrency representing proceeds of cyber criminal activity, and disrupted a criminal cyber network through a joint operation with international partners. // FBI SWAT personnel are located in the background. // A white FBI logo is in the lower right corner.

FBI Anchorage in 2025 served hundreds of crime victims across Alaska; reached over 4,000 youth, including over 3,700 youth on internet safety and sextortion awareness; and reached over 1,200 seniors and community members with elder fraud prevention and awareness presentations. // In the background, an FBI employee is shown presenting to students. // A white FBI logo is in the lower right corner.

tweets.text:
As we welcome 2026, look back at how @FBISanJuan helped protect its communities and keep Americans safe in 2025. 

"As the year comes to a close, the FBI remains focused on its core priorities: crushing violent crime, defending the homeland, rebuilding public trust, and maintaining fierce organizational accountability. In 2025, while partnering with local, federal, and territorial law enforcement - and working alongside the communities of Puerto Rico and the US Virgin Islands - FBI San Juan executed 381 arrests, located 24 child victims, conducted 105 drug seizures, and seized over $26 million in criminal assets.  We stand committed to the safety of our communities and believe that together we will continue to achieve amazing results in 2026."

As we welcome 2026, look back at how @FBIBuffalo helped protect its communities and keep Americans safe in 2025. 

"#FBI Buffalo is highlighting our 2025 efforts to crush violent crime and protect communities throughout Western New York. During 2025, FBI Buffalo made over 600 arrests, leading the nation with a 400% increase in violent crime arrests compared to 2024. As we enter 2026, FBI Buffalo remains dedicated to protecting the American people by leveraging our law enforcement partnerships to remove dangerous criminals, weapons, and illegal drugs from our communities. From #YourFBI: Thank you for your support. We wish everyone a joyful and safe 2026!"

As we welcome 2026, here's how @FBILosAngeles helped protect its communities and keep Americans safe in 2025. 

"Akil Davis, Assistant Director in Charge of FBI Los Angeles, brings to a close 2025 by highlighting this year's investigative successes which include the dismantling of state-sponsored hactivists and the takedown of a violent extremist group. ADIC Davis also noted the apprehension of over 260 fugitives and recovering nearly 70 children from potential trafficking and exploitation. Operational achievements such as "Sinaloa September", "Operation Summer Heat", and "Operation Coast to Coast" yielded hundreds of arrests and significant seizures of illegal drugs, guns and approximately $40 million in high-end motorcycles."

tweets.text:
As we welcome 2026, look back at how @NewYorkFBI helped protect its communities and keep Americans safe in 2025. 

"The New York Field Office worked with our federal, state, and local partners to help keep our communities safe this year resulting in more than 1,800 arrests, nearly 500 sentencings, 460 convictions, 400 indictments, and has seized nearly $574 million in forfeitures. 

The office surged resources during two nationwide initiatives orchestrated to drive the violent crime rate down across the country: 

🚨During Operation Summer Heat, FBI New York conducted more than 470 arrests, 81 convictions and sentencings, and seized nearly 100 drugs and weapons. 

🚨To round out the year, our agents and NYPD Task Force Officers conducted more than 60 arrests, seized nearly a dozen illegal firearms, and recovered a substantial amount of cocaine, fentanyl, and meth as a part of Operation Coast to Coast. 

As we move into 2026, the FBI will continue to prioritize crushing violent crime, defending the homeland, rebuilding public trust, and fostering organizational accountability."

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBIMilwaukee: A Wisconsin man has pleaded guilty to wire fraud and money laundering in connection to his long-term investment fraud scheme. Corey Kromray admitted that between 2018 and 2025, he used social media to falsely portray himself as a successful trader and to display wealth he allegedly acquired from trading, including images or references to expensive watches, a large home, tropical vacations, and exotic sports cars. Instead of investing money as promised, Kromray often used investor money to pay his personal expenses.

Read more about the joint #FBI and IRS investigation: https://t.co/W10SEe5zCt

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBICharlotte: North Carolina Truck Driver Charged Federally After Bank Robbery and Shootout with Arresting Officers

According to the complaint, on December 26, Willie Edward Mcgee, Jr. entered a bank and demanded money. The teller gave Mcgee cash, and surveillance footage depicted Mcgee leaving the scene in a semi-truck. Later that day, Rocky Mount Police located and were surveilling the truck when Mcgee exited armed with a rifle and began firing at the officers. 

Learn more details about the investigation by the #FBI and partners: https://t.co/rhJHZd8HLP

The teller gave Mcgee cash, and surveillance footage depicted Mcgee leaving the scene in a semi-truck. Later that day, Rocky Mount Police located and were surveilling the truck when Mcgee exited armed with a rifle and began firing at the officers.

tweets.text:
As the year comes to a close, here's how @FBIAlbuquerque helped protect their communities and keep Americans safe in 2025. 

"#FBI-Albuquerque has worked alongside federal, state, tribal and local partners throughout the past year to help protect New Mexico. By increasing joint operations and arrests we will continue to target violent crime and remove criminals from our streets. But we couldn't do this without you. Community members play a vital role in this effort by reporting tips. No matter where you live, we are committed to your safety. As we move into 2026, FBI Albuquerque will remain focused on crushing violent crime, defending the homeland, rebuilding public trust, and maintaining organizational accountability. "

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @NewYorkFBI: Cholo Abdi Abdullah Sentenced to Life in Prison for Conspiring to Commit 9/11-Style Terrorist Attack on Behalf Of al-Shabaab

This week, Cholo Abdi Abdullah was sentenced to life in prison for conspiring to provide, and providing, material support to a foreign terrorist organization, and conspiring to murder U.S. nationals, commit aircraft piracy, destroy aircraft, and commit transnational acts of terrorism.  

Harakat al-Shabaab al-Mujahideen, commonly known as al-Shabaab, is a terrorist organization and al Qaeda affiliate based in Somalia and active in other locations in East Africa. Abdullah joined al-Shabaab in 2015 and was recruited by senior al-Shabaab operatives for a “greater plan,” one that was “bigger than the fighting and the explosives.”  Specifically, Abdullah agreed to join al-Shabaab’s international scheme to execute a mass-casualty terrorist attack, which would involve training to become an airline pilot so that he could hijack a commercial plane and crash it into a building in the U.S.

The #FBI Joint Terrorism Task Force led the investigation in Abdullah, seen here at flight school and at his extradition to the U.S. to face justice. Learn more here: https://t.co/r70VQTpGHP

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBISanAntonio: A 19-year-old Texas-based leader of the Nihilistic Violent Extremist group "764" has pleaded guilty to RICO and child exploitation.

Alexis Aldair Chavez was a leader of an online network where members worked towards a common purpose of destroying civilized society through the corruption and exploitation of vulnerable populations, including minors. Chavez and co-conspirators groomed and extorted minor victims to engage in a variety of violent conduct, including self-mutilation and other egregious acts.

The #FBI remains vigilant against the threat of NVE networks that operate within the U.S. and around the globe. Read more about this investigation and our efforts to protect children here: https://t.co/GWZvPsxz4t

tweets.text:
This holiday season, the FBI's Criminal Justice Information Services Division is working 24/7/365 to provide law enforcement with the critical systems they need to protect the American people. #FBI staff are also out in the community connecting with you. 

We are dedicated to helping youth in need and supporting the community with services such as free child ID fingerprinting. Fingerprints are proven to be one of the most effective tools for law enforcement to locate and identify missing family members. Although the FBI does not keep these voluntary records, we provide copies to parents for future use. 

#YourFBI is committed to strengthening your trust with us. Learn more about our community outreach efforts at https://t.co/h0dipDi2K6.

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBINewOrleans: Member of Anti-Capitalist and Anti-Government Group Arrested and Charged with Threatening ICE Officers

According to court documents, Micah James Legnon is associated with the Turtle Island Liberation Front (TILF), a far-left, pro-Palestine, anti-government, and anti-capitalist group. Legnon was under surveillance by the FBI on Dec. 12, due to his affiliation with TILF members who were arrested that day for plotting to bomb Los Angeles. FBI saw Legnon leaving his New Iberia residence with what appeared to be an assault rifle and body armor in his vehicle in an apparent effort to execute an attack. Legnon was stopped and arrested by the Terrebonne Parish Sheriff’s Office, and one assault rifle, one pistol, one gas canister, and body armor were recovered from his vehicle. 

Read more about the ongoing #FBI investigation at https://t.co/qJJ7zBNiZN.

CASE UPDATE from @FBIMilwaukee: Louis A. Tucker, 23, and Nyomi L.M. Acosta, 21, were indicted by a federal grand jury two counts for their alleged involvement in child abuse and child neglect on the Menominee Indian Reservation.  

According to the indictment, the defendants recklessly caused great bodily harm to a child by striking the child about the head and body, fracturing the child’s left radius and ulna, burning the child’s back and torso, and negligently failed to provide medical care for the child.

Read more about the #FBI investigation in coordination with partners: https://t.co/ng7v9lqUkK

tweets.text:
NEWS ALERT from @FBIBillings and @FBISaltLakeCity: Man Charged with Selling Fraudulent Identity Documents Used by Cybercriminals

Zahid Hasan of Bangladesh has been charged with operating illegal online marketplaces that sold digital templates of false identity documents, such as passports, social security cards, and driver’s licenses. For example, customers could use virtual currencies to purchase a U.S. passport template for the equivalent of $12. The #FBI and our partners seized three domains operated by Hasan: https://t.co/lNwwPeAqsg; https://t.co/TOL5XR9ADH; and https://t.co/JijgcTR1VQ. Hasan is alleged to have received more than $2.9 million through sales on TechTreek from more than 1,400 customers located around the world.

Read more about the #FBI Cyber Task Force investigation: https://t.co/RlpwwYOUeI

NEWS ALERT from @FBIChicago: A federal grand jury has indicted six alleged members and associates of a Chicago street gang for participating in a criminal organization that murdered rivals, committed armed robberies and carjackings, and violently protected their perceived territory on the South Side of Chicago. 

The indictment alleges that members of the Faceworld street gang engaged in numerous acts of violence, including 13 murders and numerous attempted murders, assaults, armed robberies, and carjackings in Chicago.  Among the violence charged in the indictment were shootings at passengers aboard a Chicago Transit Authority bus and at mourners attending the funeral of a rival gang member.

Read more about the investigation and efforts by the #FBI and our law enforcement partners to crush violent crime in cities across the country: https://t.co/QhEcbN7HpV

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE FROM @NewYorkFBI: Four MS-13 gang members were convicted of racketeering and murder charges. Edenilson Velasquez Larin, Hugo Diaz Amaya, Jose Espinoza Sanchez, and Jose Arevalao Iraheta were found responsible for orchestrating and facilitating four murders on behalf of the gang in Queens and on Long Island. 

As proved at trial, MS-13 is a violent street gang operating through “cliques” or chapters in Queens, Long Island and communities across the United States and beyond. The gang primarily makes money through drug trafficking and extortion and has been responsible for dozens of murders in the Eastern District of New York alone.

Learn more about the efforts by the #FBI and our partners to eradicate gang violence: https://t.co/RJXpBVCfqH

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBINorfolk: Man sentenced to 10 years in prison for coercion and enticement of a minor

According to court documents, Amery Muniz, 21, was engaged in the sale and distribution of child sexual abuse material (CSAM) and encouraged a 12-year-old victim to produce and send CSAM to him, at one point offering to send the victim $50. Investigators identified approximately 800 CSAM images on Muniz’s phone.

Read more about the #FBI investigation and Project Safe Childhood: https://t.co/gddbUOR1R4

tweets.text:
Malicious actors are impersonating senior U.S. officials via text and AI-generated voice messages and targeting their contacts, including family members.

🚨 In this scheme, actors reach out to victims and engage them in a brief conversation on a topic they are knowledgeable about. They quickly request to move the discussion to a secondary, encrypted messaging app.

🚨 If contacted by someone you know via a new platform or phone number, verify the new contact information through a previously confirmed platform or trusted source.

Read the #FBI’s new #PSA for tips to spot a fake message and protect yourself from this campaign: https://t.co/1ZkyAtLHPe

NEWS ALERT from @FBISpringfield: "Operation Safe Christmas" results in more than 60 criminal arrests over seven days. 

The #FBI in coordination with federal, state, and local law enforcement partners conducted a week-long fugitive apprehension operation that resulted in 63 arrests of criminal offenders, and the seizure of 2.5 pounds of narcotics and $15,000.  The arrests took place across Illinois and in Atlanta, Ga, and included man charged with shooting police officer.

Read more: https://t.co/Ce9kSPP1FF

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBIChicago: Chicago Street Gang Leader Convicted of Participating in Racketeering Conspiracy Involving Multiple Murders

Following a six-week federal trial, a leader of the Four Corner Hustlers street gang has been convicted of participating in a criminal organization that terrorized the West Side of Chicago for two decades by committing murders, robberies, extortions, witness tampering, and drug dealing. The jury found that Labar Spann, 47, committed a total of four murders in a premediated manner as part of the racketeering conspiracy.

Read more about the #FBI investigation at https://t.co/WepyeosVPd

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBICharlotte: North Carolina Drug Kingpin Sentenced to 50 Years in Prison

According to court records, Akeem Netron Holder Evans, 36, operated a long-running drug trafficking organization out of multiple residences in Raleigh and the surrounding area. As part of the operation, Evans exploited personal relationships, convincing his former childhood football coach to transport kilogram quantities of cocaine. Evans and his co-conspirators used stash houses, encrypted communications, and counter measures in an attempt to evade law enforcement. 

Read more about the joint investigation at https://t.co/KqqygF1VJc

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBICincinnatti: Following an investigation by the #FBI and IRS, an Ohio man was sentenced to 54 months in federal prison for defrauding elderly victims.

According to court documents, while employed by a life insurance brokerage, Ronald Scott Daley convinced client victims to withdraw assets from the insurance brokerage and deposit the funds into their personal accounts. Daley then influenced the victims to make payments to a bank account for an entity that Daley controlled. Daley defrauded three victims of more than $707,000 total.

Read more about the investigation: https://t.co/x8XL6ER86D

tweets.text:
FBI agents and analysts are working closely with law enforcement partners to track leads and gather intelligence to identify the person responsible for the mass shooting that occurred at Brown University. Members of the #FBI Evidence Response Team and specialists from the FBI Laboratory are also assisting in processing and documenting evidence from the scene. As the investigation develops, the FBI will continue to provide assets, resources, and personnel to support our partners in seeking justice for the victims and their loved ones.

On Monday, @FBIBoston joined @ProvidenceRIPD, @ristatepolice, and other law enforcement partners to announce a $50,000 reward for information leading to the identification, arrest, and conviction of the individual responsible. If you have any information that could advance the investigation, contact the FBI at 1-800-CALL-FBI. 

View images of the individual here: https://t.co/ceNGiaOutZ

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBIAlbuquerque: This week, a California woman was sentenced to 30 months in prison and ordered to pay more than $1 million in restitution for orchestrating a fraud scheme. 

According to court records, from 2001 to 2023, Lanette Bashore falsely claimed to own or control a multi-million-dollar fortune that was tied up by various financial barriers. Bashore convinced individuals to send her thousands of dollars up front to “unlock” the purported fortune, promising to repay them. In reality, no such fortune existed. Bashore instead used the money to fund her personal lifestyle and gambling.

Learn more about the #FBI investigation: https://t.co/QhxlASvMpe

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBIChicago: A man has been sentenced to a decade in federal prison for illegally possessing a loaded handgun and using it to shoot a man multiple times in Chicago.

On the evening of June 8, 2021, Angel Sosa, 27, fired more than a dozen shots at a man in the Noble Square neighborhood of Chicago. The man was seriously wounded but survived. Sosa had previously been convicted of multiple felonies and was on parole at the time. He pleaded guilty earlier this year. 

Read more about the #FBI investigation in partnership with the Chicago Police Department: https://t.co/5Wp2T1YWUL

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBISeattle: Washington man who paid for worker to be smuggled from Mexico pleads guilty: Victim was not paid, escaped, and was treated for injuries

According to records, Rangel Ramirez-Manzano, 57, knew the victim and paid $17,000 for her to be smuggled into the U.S. Upon arrival, he harbored her in his home and forced her to begin hard labor for his landscaping business. He also threatened her, restricted her food, and failed to pay her wages. The victim escaped in 2022 and was treated at a hospital for injuries. The #FBI and partners arrested Ramirez-Manzano, and he has remained in federal custody since.

Read more about the case and #FBI investigation here: https://t.co/sHpCF29Hqp

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBIBaltimore: Three defendants have been sentenced to prison in connection with a kidnapping and murder committed by members of the Shotgun Crips gang.

According to court documents and evidence presented at trial, the Shotgun Crips targeted the 35-year-old victim following a dispute. Kimon Burton-Roberson, 32, who executed the victim by shooting him in the head with an assault rifle, received a life sentence. Jamil Salahuddin, 22, who helped abduct the victim and beat him, received a sentence of 17.5 years in prison.  Stephanie Bultes-Ramirez, 27, who drove the vehicle in which the victim was transported to the murder scene received a sentence of 10 years in prison.  

Read more about the joint investigation by #FBI Baltimore and partners: https://t.co/KG4NMPSbR5

tweets.text:
MS-13 Leader Wanted for Assassination of Four People in Honduras Arrested in Nebraska

Gerson Emir Cuadra Soto was a leader in MS-13 who was part of a professional assassination squad. Cuadra Soto is alleged to have entered the U.S. illegally in 2022 and was arrested without incident on December 8, 2025.

“Hiding in the heart of the heartland is not a good option for violent offenders; we will turn over every stone to find you. The apprehension of Cuadra Soto is a testament to the power of our partnerships; we are grateful to @FBIHouston and Joint Task Force Vulcan, the TRIDENT Task Force, the Grand Island Police Department, and CBP’s BORTAC team.” - FBI Omaha Special Agent in Charge Eugene Kowel

Read more from #FBI Omaha: https://t.co/0YCypBepGe

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBIDenver: Serial Denver Bank Robber Sentenced to 210 Months in Prison

According to the facts established at trial, Flozell Beasley, 73, robbed two banks, two credit unions, and a taco shop in Denver in 2023. In each robbery, he dressed like a construction worker and threatened employees with a high-quality replica gun before taking their money and fleeing. Beasley was identified and arrested by the #FBI Rocky Mountain Safe Streets Task Force and partners.

Read more about the investigation: https://t.co/ilSVG0vOGL

CASE UPDATE from @FBIBoston: A California man has pleaded guilty for his role in a scheme that defrauded companies, individuals, and the Town of Bristol, Rhode Island, out of nearly $9 million. 

According to court documents, Alec Tahir Baker, 61, used phishing and spoofed vendor emails to gain access to victim computer networks and redirect wire transfers into bank accounts that he controlled. He then rapidly withdrew, transferred, or layered the funds to disguise their origin.

Read more about the #FBI investigation: https://t.co/1H2yjSAYwZ

This week, @FBILosAngeles released a newly obtained photograph of #FBI Top Ten Fugitive Ryan James Wedding. The photo is believed to have been taken in Mexico during the summer of 2025. 
 
Wedding is #wanted for allegedly running and participating in a transnational drug trafficking operation and orchestrating multiple murders in furtherance of drug crimes. The U.S. is offering a #reward of up to $15 million for information leading to his arrest and/or conviction.

tweets.text:
Father and Son Leaders of Phoenix-Based Transnational Criminal Organization Sentenced to Prison for Violations of Kingpin Statute and Money Laundering

Marcos Monarrez Jr., 26, of Phoenix, was sentenced to life in prison, and his father, Marcos Monarrez-Mendoza, 55, of Mexico, was sentenced to 15 years to be followed by deportation to Mexico. They were among 35 individuals charged in 2024 for their participation in a domestic and international narcotics and money laundering conspiracy involving substantial quantities of fentanyl, methamphetamine, and cocaine.

Read more from @FBIPittsburgh: https://t.co/oHxzFv6mlQ.

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBISanDIego: Wife of Sinaloa Cartel Kingpin Sentenced to Prison

Claudia Patricia Alvarez Hernandez, the wife of incarcerated Sinaloa Cartel kingpin Jorge Alberto Valenzuela Valenzuela, was sentenced to 14 years in prison this week for her role in a massive drug trafficking and money laundering conspiracy. 

Investigators determined that Jorge rose from a low-level drug trafficker to a top Sinaloa Cartel drug kingpin. As part of her relationship with Jorge, Alvarez Hernandez was able to live in luxury based on the massive amount of cocaine that Jorge and his organization trafficked.

Read more about the investigation: https://t.co/QH32ZgLU38

tweets.text:
CASE UPDATE from @FBIKansasCity: This week, a federal jury found two U.S. citizens guilty of providing material support to separatist fighters Cameroon.

Francis Chenyi, Sr., 52, of Minnesota, and Lah Nestor Langmi, 49, of New York, both U.S. citizens of Cameroonian origin, were convicted Tuesday for their roles in a conspiracy to provide support or resources to kill, kidnap, and maim persons and use weapons of mass destruction in a foreign country. The defendants conspired to provide funds and equipment to separatist fighters to allow them to construct and use IEDs against various targets in the Northwest Region of Cameroon.

Read more about the #FBI investigation: https://t.co/yTEwQtS3RW
